Name Data Type Access Description Default Version
InternetGatewayDevice. Object R
- 6.20
DeviceSummary string(1024) R 
Profiles according TR-098A3 which are currently supported for a FON WLAN USB-Host Box:
"InternetGatewayDevice:1.4[](Baseline:2, EthernetLAN:1, Time:2,  ADSLWAN:1, IPPing:1, WiFiLAN:1, DeviceAssociation:1)
VoiceService 1.0[1] (SIPEndpoint:1, Endpoint:1, TAEndpoint:1)
StorageService:1.0[1](FTPServer:1, NetServer:1,UserAccess:1,VolumeConfig:1)".
On special F!Box hardware (UR8) support of "ADSL2WAN" profile.
 - 6.20

 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ProtocolPrefer int RW 
Only relevant if both IPv4 and IPv6 are available at runtime: This parameter defines the preference of IPv4 or IPv6 usage for the TR-069 protocol. Allowed values are:  0:ipv4_only, 1:ipv6_only, 2:ipv4_prefer, 3:ipv6_prefer, 4:ip_both. Default is ipv4_prefer.
 2 8.10
X_AVM-DE_TR069FirmwareDownloadEnabled boolean RW 
Enables/disables the tr069-download-request (necessary for a firmware-update with tr-069)

Write Access is OEM specific
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_FirmwareUpdatePrecheck boolean RW 
FRITZ!OS verifies the existence of a requested firmware update file via an HTTP HEAD request.
This mechanism can be disabled or enabled through this parameter.
 - 7.50
X_AVM-DE_SessionTerminationWithEmptyPost boolean RW 
CPE sends empty HTTP Post before terminating session. See 3.7.1.3 TR-069 A3.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_STB_URL string RW 
DHCP option 43.1 in local LAN
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_STB_ProvCode string RW 
DHCP option 43.2 in local LAN
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_STB_Matchoption string RW 
Matchoption for connected LAN-Devices to use DHCP 43 in LAN
 dhcpoption 60 *dslforum.org* 7.20
X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestEnable boolean RW 
Enables or disables the ConnectionRequest port.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestSecurityEnable boolean RW 
Enables or disables a security mechanism for TR-069 connection-requests which means that
these requests are only permitted for clients with IP addresses resulting from the DNS resolution (IPv4/IPv6) of the ManagementServerURL or as an alternative
from the IP addresses/subnets supplied in parameter "X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestIPNetList".
The dns resolution will be performed with the initialization of the connection request socket and from then on in a periodic interval specified in parameter 'X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestDNSUpdateTimeInterval'.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestIPNetList string(1024) RW 
Supplies a comma-seperated list of ip-addresses or ip-subnets (IPv4/IPv6) from which a connection-request will be allowed.
(Example:  "172.17.1.13,172.16.0.1/16,2001:db8::/64" - means that connection requests are allowed from one single ip-address and two subnets)

Note: Will be only used if parameter "X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestSecurityEnable" is set to true (1).
Providing an empty list while former parameter is set, means that the DNS resolution mechanism is used for securing the connection-request port.

Note: In FRITZ!OS versions <= 07.27 just subnets with prefix_len of 64 are supported for IPv6.
For IPv4 all subnets and also single addresses are supported without hesitation.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestDNSUpdateTimeInterval unsignedInt[10:86400] RW 
Unit: seconds. Sets the periodic interval for the dns resoultion of the acs server.
Note: Only used if parameter X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestSecurityEnable is set to true (1) and parameter X_AVM-DE_ConnectionRequestIPNetList has been left empty.

Default value is 86400 seconds (== one day).
 86400 7.00
X_AVM-DE_VerifyServer boolean RW 
Enables or disables server certificate check for TLS-secured TR-069 connections and HTTPS downloads.
Has no effect on unencrypted connections.
 - 7.20
X_AVM-DE_UseCableModemInterface boolean RW 
As soon as this value is set (== 1), the tr069 connection will be established via the CM interface of the cable box.

Please take note of the following guidelines:

- Changing this value results in a reboot.
- If the feature is activated, the ACS url must also be an HTTP url with an IP address 
  (HTTPs and DNS resolution are NOT available via the CM interface)
 false 7.20
X_AVM-DE_WiFiEnvironmentUploadEnable boolean RW 
Allow the ACS to upload the WiFi environment via TR-069 (type "X 00040E WiFi Environment").
Cannot be disabled after enabling.
Cannot be enabled if the feature has been manually disabled by the user.
Enabling WiFi environment uploads triggers an event in the F!Box event log.
 false 7.20
X_AVM-DE_USPControllerAddAllowed boolean R 
This parameter is controlled by the user via the GUI. If true, the user grants access to add USP controllers through the ACS. If false, the ACS is not allowed to add USP controllers and any existing USP controllers added by the ACS are removed.
 true 7.50
X_AVM-DE_USPControllerNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
Number of entries in the  table.
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.ManagementServer.X_AVM-DE_USPController.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: EndpointID.
 - 7.50
Enable boolean RW 
Enable/Disable this InternetGatewayDevice.ManagementServer.X_AVM-DE_USPController.{i}. instance.
 false 7.50
UseTLS boolean RW 
TLS enabled/disabled 
Must always be set to true. The default  fbox certificate store is used to verify the server.
 true 7.50
EndpointID string(64) RW 
Endpoint Identifier (The unique USP identifier for this USP Controller.)

Endpoints are identified by an Endpoint Identifier.
The Endpoint Identifier is a locally or globally unique USP layer identifier of an Endpoint. Whether it is globally or locally unique depends on the scheme used for assignment.
The Endpoint Identifier (ID) is used in the USP Record and various Parameters in a USP Message to uniquely identify Controller and Agent Endpoints. It can be globally or locally unique, either among all Endpoints or among all Controllers or all Agents, depending on the scheme used for assignment.
The Endpoint ID is comprised of two mandatory and one optionally mandatory components: authority-scheme, authority-id, and instance-id.

These three components are combined as:

authority-scheme ":" [authority-id] ":" instance-id

The format of the authority-id is dictated by the authority-scheme. The format of the instance-id is dictated either by the authority-scheme or by the entity identified by the authority-id.
When used in a certificate, an Endpoint ID is expressed as a urn in the bbf namespace as:
"urn:bbf:usp:id:" authority-scheme ":" [authority-id] ":" instance-id

When used anywhere else (e.g. in the to_id and from_id of a USP Record), the namespace information is omitted, and the Endpoint ID is expressed as:
authority-scheme ":" [authority-id] ":" instance-id
 - 7.50
Host string(255) RW 
The controller hostname or IP address
 - 7.50
Username string(256) RW 
Authentication Data (Websocket: supported: HTTP-Basic Auth and HTTP Digest-Auth.)
 - 7.50
Password string(256) RW 
Authentication Data (Websocket: supported: HTTP-Basic Auth and HTTP Digest-Auth.). Reading this parameter returns an empty string,
 - 7.50
Path string(1024) RW 
The path on the controller.
Only affects Controllers that use WebSockets as MTP.
 <empty> 7.50
Port unsignedInt[1:65535] RW 
The port that the controller is listening on
 - 7.50
MTP string RW 
Specifies the message transfer protocol
Enumeration of:
- websocket
- mqtt
 - 7.50
ProvisioningCode string(64) RW 
Identifying information which MAY be used by the InternetGatewayDevice.ManagementServer.X_AVM-DE_USPController.{i}. instance to determine InternetGatewayDevice.ManagementServer.X_AVM-DE_USPController.{i}. instance specific customization and provisioning parameters.
 - 7.50
UseTR069ClientCert boolean RW 
This parameter is only displayed if the underlying tr069 session is secured with a TR069 client certificate.
If this is the case, you can use this parameter (true) to reuse the certificate as a client certificate for the connection to the usp controller.
 false 7.50
MQTTControllerTopic string(1024) RW 
The Controller MQTT topic name, meaning the destination for messages sent by the Agent to the Controller.
Only affects Controllers that use MQTT as MTP.
 - 7.90
MQTTResponseTopic string(1024) RW 
The configured response topic, meaning the destination for messages sent by the Controller to the Agent.
The placeholder [[EID]] for the Agent's endpoint ID may be used in order to create unique topics.
For instance, writing the value "/topics/usp/[[EID]]" will cause the Agent to try to subscribe to a topic named "/topic/usp/os::00040E-9988776655AB".
Only affects Controllers that use MQTT as MTP.
 - 7.90
MQTTClientID string(1024) RW 
The MQTT client identifier used in the MQTT CONNECT packet.
 - 8.01
InternetGatewayDevice.ManagementServer.X_AVM-DE_USPController.{i}.AccessRights. Object RW 
Specifies access rights of a usp controller (will show/hide nodes in the agents data model)
 - 7.50
WiFi boolean RW

 false 7.50
Controller boolean RW 
Access right allowing the Controller to create additional Controller instances

Mesh boolean RW

 false 7.50
Internet boolean RW 
Access right for all object related to the device internet connection, such as Device.PPP. or Device.DNS.
 false 7.50
System boolean RW 
Access right for all system operations, such as firmware updates, reboots or factory resets
 false 7.50
VoIP boolean RW 
Access right for all Voice over IP related operations in Device.Services.VoiceService.
 false 7.50

InternetGatewayDevice.UserInterface. Object R
- 6.20
UpgradeAvailable boolean RW 
true if F!Box has been detected a firmware update on a AVM server.
 - 6.20
AutoUpdateServer string(256) RW
- 6.20
AvailableLanguages string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. only available on Boxes with MULTI_LANGUAGE support.
 - 6.20
CurrentLanguage string(16) RW 
only available on Boxes with MULTI_LANGUAGE support. Reboot required.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_FirmwareUpdateRequest string RW 
Set to "1" to request F!Box performing a firmware update from a AVM server. A firmware update is performed only if "UpgradeAvailable" AND "X_AVM-DE_TR069FirmwareDownloadEnabled" are true.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_AlternativeISPDisplayname string RW 
Setting this parameter will overwrite the existing ISP-Providername with an alternative name. Affects only display purposes in the WebUI.
 - 7.90
X_AVM-DE_AllowBackgroundCommWithManufacturer boolean R 
If this parameter is false, no update, status and monitoring requests will be sent from F!OS to the manufacturer.
 - 7.90
X_AVM-DE_AutoUpdate int RW 
Note: This parameter is only relevant if X_AVM_DE-AllowBackgroundCommWithManufacturer = true AND UpgradesManaged = false
Activate automatic firmware udpates. Allowed values are:
0: disabled   [DEPRECATED - use IGD.ManagementServer.UpgradesManaged instead ]
1: check only for available firmware updates
2: check and install important firmware updates
3: check and install all firmware updates
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_AutoUpdateTime string R 
As soon as an update is available and has been recognized by the FRITZ!Box, the calculated AutoUpdateTime on which the update is carried out is returned.
If no update is available an empty value is returned.
Note: The time format is the local time of FRITZ!Box configuration (see: InternetGatewayDevice.Time.LocalTimeZoneName)
Possible Patterns:
- DD.MM.YYYY hh:mm
- DD.MM.YYYY h:mm
 - 7.20

 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW

 HTTPS 6.20

InternetGatewayDevice.UserInterface.X_AVM-DE_MaintenanceWindow. Object R
- 7.20
Enabled boolean R 
Indicates whether the user has activated the MaintenanceWindow for updates in the user interface or not
 false 7.20
Userdefined boolean R 
Indicates whether the user has edited the start time of the MaintenanceWindow for updates in the user interface or not
 false 7.20
Start unsignedInt[0:1439] R 
Unit: minutes. Returns the start time of the MaintenanceWindow as the number of minutes from midnight.
Depending on the parameter X_AVM-DE_MaintenanceWindow.Userdefined, this value was automatically determined or set by the user.
Notes:
- The length of the MaintenanceWindow is fixed: 3 hours
- This parameter just takes effect if X_AVM-DE_MaintenanceWindow.Enabled is "true"
 - 7.20

InternetGatewayDevice.LANConfigSecurity. Object R
- 6.20
ConfigPassword string(8:32) RW 
Set a GUI password for the CPE. This is only allowed if the CPE is unprotected and the user did not set expressly this authorisation method. To check whether the box is protected by any login method, use the parameter X_AVM-DE_BoxAuthRequired.  ConfigPassword must have a min lenght of 8 and a max lenght of 32 characters. When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_BoxAuthRequired boolean R 
Could be used to check whether the FRITZ!Box is protected by a login method (password or user account).
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_ResetBoxAuth boolean RW 
'Password forgotten' service: Setting X_AVM-DE_ResetBoxAuth to 'true' starts a time windows of 300 seconds. Withhin this time window user will have the opportunity to set a new password for the FRITZ!Box Web UI.
Allowed Value: true.
 - 6.80
InternetGatewayDevice.IPPingDiagnostics. Object R
- 6.20
AverageResponseTime unsignedInt R 
Unit: milliseconds. Result parameter indicating the average response time over all repetitions with successful responses of the most recent ping test.
If there were no successful responses, this value MUST be zero.
 - 6.20
ProtocolVersion string RW 
Describes rather IPv4 or IPv6 is used
Enumeration of:
- IPv4
- IPv6
 IPv4 6.20
DataBlockSize unsignedInt[1:65535] RW 
Unit: bytes. Size of the data block to be sent for each ping
 - 6.20
DiagnosticsState string RW 
Indicates availability of diagnostic data.
If the ACS sets the value of this parameter to {{enum|Requested}}, the CPE MUST initiate the corresponding diagnostic test.
When writing, the only allowed value is {{enum|Requested}}.
Enumeration of:
- None
- Requested
- Complete
- Error_CannotResolveHostName
- Error_Internal
- Error_NoRouteToHost
 - 6.20
DSCP unsignedInt[0:64] RW 
Functionality not implemented (placeholder)
 - 6.20
FailureCount unsignedInt R 
Result parameter indicating the number of failed pings in the most recent ping test.
 - 6.20
Host string(256) RW 
Host name or address of the host to ping.
 - 6.20
Interface string(256) RW 
Functionality not implemented (placeholder) The value MUST be the path name of a table row.
 - 6.20
MaximumResponseTime unsignedInt R 
Unit: milliseconds. Result parameter indicating the maximum response time over all repetitions with successful responses of the most recent ping test.
If there were no successful responses, this value MUST be zero.
 - 6.20
MinimumResponseTime unsignedInt R 
Unit: milliseconds. Result parameter indicating the minimum response time over all repetitions with successful responses of the most recent ping test.
If there were no successful responses, this value MUST be zero.
 - 6.20
NumberOfRepetitions unsignedInt[1:] RW 
Number of repetitions of the ping test to perform before reporting the results.
 - 6.20
SuccessCount unsignedInt R 
Result parameter indicating the number of successful pings (those in which a successful response was received prior to the timeout) in the most recent ping test.
 - 6.20
Timeout unsignedInt RW 
Functionality not implemented (placeholder)
 - 6.20

 - 6.20
Enable boolean R 
return always true
 - 6.20
MACAddress MACAddress R
- 6.20
MACAddressControlEnabled boolean R 
returns always false
 - 6.20
MaxBitRate string R 
returns always "Auto"

Name string(16) R
- 6.20
Status string R 
Enumeration of:
- Up
- NoLink
- Error (OPTIONAL)
- Disabled

DEPRECATED. Do not use parameter X_AVM-DE_EEEForAllPortsDisabled anymore.

InternetGatewayDevice.LANDevice.{i}.LANEthernetInterfaceConfig.{i}.X_AVM-DE_EthPort.{i}. Object R
- 6.20
Name string R 
Returns "LAN1"... "LAN4"
 - 6.20
Linkstate string R 
Returns "up" or "disconnected"
 - 6.20
Powermode string R 
Returns "auto" or "green".
 - 6.20
Speed unsignedInt R 
Delivers speed of LAN port in MB/s.
 - 7.00
PossiblePortmodes string R 
List of portmodes (LAN, WAN, Guest) supported by this Ethernet port
 - 7.20
Portmode string RW 
Current portmode (LAN, WAN, Guest) of this Ethernet port
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.LANDevice.{i}.WLANConfiguration.{i}. Object R 
At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: BSSID, SSID.
 - 6.20
AutoChannelEnable boolean RW 
only if CPE supports Autochannel
 - 6.20
AutoRateFallBackEnabled boolean RW 
F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
BasicAuthenticationMode string RW 
fixed value 'none'.  Write access with values not equal 'none' will be responded with error.
Enumeration of:
- None (Open authentication)
- EAPAuthentication (OPTIONAL)
- SharedAuthentication (OPTIONAL)
 - 6.20
BasicDataTransmitRates string(256) RW 
Comma-separated list. F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
BasicEncryptionModes string RW 
if encryption not equal 'none', then 'WEPEncryption' is returned. Write Access not supported.
Enumeration of:
- None
- WEPEncryption
 - 6.20
BeaconAdvertisementEnabled boolean RW 
optional parameter: corresponds to "SSID invisible"
 - 6.20
BeaconType string RW 
determines used WLAN encrytion (none, WEP, WPA, WPA2).
Enumeration of:
- None
- Basic
- WPA
- 11i (OPTIONAL)
- BasicandWPA (OPTIONAL)
- Basicand11i (OPTIONAL)
- WPAand11i (OPTIONAL)
- BasicandWPAand11i (OPTIONAL)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_PossibleBeaconTypes string R 
Delivers possible Beacon Types of the device in a comma separated list.
Enumeration of:
- None
- WPA
- 11i
- WPAand11i
- WPA3
- 11iandWPA3
 - 7.20
BSSID string R
- 6.20
Channel unsignedInt[0:255] RW
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
IEEE11iAuthenticationMode string RW 
fixed value "PSKAuthentication". Write access with values not equal "PSKAuthentication" will be responded with error.
Enumeration of:
- PSKAuthentication
- EAPAuthentication (OPTIONAL)
- EAPandPSKAuthentication (OPTIONAL)
 - 6.20
IEEE11iEncryptionModes string RW 
fixed value "AESEncryption". Write access with values not equal "AESEncryption" will be responded with error.
Enumeration of:
- WEPEncryption
- TKIPEncryption (OPTIONAL)
- WEPandTKIPEncryption
- AESEncryption
- WEPandAESEncryption (OPTIONAL)
- TKIPandAESEncryption (OPTIONAL)
- WEPandTKIPandAESEncryption (OPTIONAL)
 - 6.20
KeyPassphrase string(63) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. KeyPassphrase for WEP
 - 6.20
LocationDescription string(4096) RW 
F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
MACAddressControlEnabled boolean RW 
MAC-Filter
 - 6.20
MaxBitRate string(4) RW 
F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
Name string(16) R
- 6.20
OperationalDataTransmitRates string(256) RW 
Comma-separated list. F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
PossibleChannels string(1024) R 
Comma-separated list.
 - 6.20
PossibleDataTransmitRates string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. F!Box: Parameter not supported
 - 6.20
RadioEnabled boolean RW 
returns "1"
 - 6.20
SSID string(32) RW
- 6.20
SSIDAdvertisementEnabled boolean RW
- 6.20
Standard string R 
acc. spec highest possible value is returned
Enumeration of:
- a
- b
- g (b and g clients supported)
- g-only (only g clients supported)
- n
- ac
- ax
 - 6.20
Status string R 
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Error (OPTIONAL)
- Disabled

TotalAssociations unsignedInt R
- 6.20
TotalBytesReceived unsignedInt R

TotalBytesSent unsignedInt R

TotalPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
TotalPacketsSent unsignedInt R

TransmitPower unsignedInt[0:100] RW

TransmitPowerSupported unsignedInt(64)[0:100] R 
Comma-separated list. returns String "6,12,25,50,100"

DEPRECATED
Comma-separated list. Supported key lengths.
fixed "Disabled, 40-bit, 104-bit"

WEPKeyIndex unsignedInt[1:4] RW
- 6.20
WPAAuthenticationMode string RW 
fixed value "PSKAuthentication". Write access with values not equal "TKIPEncryption" will be responded with error.

- EAPAuthentication (OPTIONAL)

fixed value 'TKIPEncryption'.  Write access with values not equal 'TKIPEncryption' will be responded with error.

- WEPandTKIPandAESEncryption (OPTIONAL)

PreSharedKey string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. use 'KeyPassphrase' to set WPA key

optional Parameter, F!Box: Parameter not supported

HostNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R

X_AVM-DE_HostTotalCnt unsignedInt R 
Total count of al known devices.

X_AVM-DE_HostlistView string RW 
Enable a filter option to get only active or inactive hosts in this object. Allowed values are: "active_only", "inactive_only", "all". X_AVM_DE_HostlistView will not be stored persistently across restarts.
 all 7.20

At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: MACAddress.

DHCP and Static

- DHCP
- Static
- AutoIP

HostName string(64) R

InterfaceType string R

WANConnectionNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R

InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANCommonInterfaceConfig. Object R
- 6.20
EnabledForInternet boolean R 
returns true
 - 6.20
WANAccessType string R 
Currently used wan accesstype
Enumeration of:
- DSL
- Ethernet (ipclient or ata)
- X_AVM-DE_Cable (DOCSIS)
- X_AVM-DE_Mobile (lte, umts or usb-stick)
- X_AVM-DE_Fiber
 - 6.20
Layer1UpstreamMaxBitRate unsignedInt R
- 6.20
Layer1DownstreamMaxBitRate unsignedInt R
- 6.20
PhysicalLinkStatus string R 
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Down
- Initializing
- Unavailable
 - 6.20
TotalBytesSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
TotalBytesReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
TotalPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
TotalPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ATA_UpstreamSpeed unsignedInt RW 
Manual configuration of the upstream bandwidth in kb/s. Setting this parameter to '0' will activate automatic detection of bandwith (automatic detection is not available in ATA Mode)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ATA_DownstreamSpeed unsignedInt RW 
Manual configuration of the downstream bandwidth in kb/s. Setting this parameter to '0' will activate automatic detection of bandwith (automatic detection is not available in ATA Mode)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_UpstreamShapedRate unsignedInt RW 
Unit: kb/s. Bandwith Regulation in kb/s.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_DownstreamShapedRate unsignedInt RW 
Unit: kb/s. Bandwith Regulation in kb/s.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_UpstreamCurrentMaxSpeed unsignedInt R 
Max. achieved throughput of the last 100 sec. in bytes/s in upstream.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_DownstreamCurrentMaxSpeed unsignedInt R 
Max. achieved throughput of the last 100 sec. in bytes/s in downstream.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_UpstreamCurrentUtilization string R 
Comma separated list of the current Utilization of upstream traffic. The measurement is made every 5 sec. All values in bytes/s.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_DownstreamCurrentUtilization string R 
Commaseparated list of the current Utilization of downstream traffic. The measurement is made every 5 sec. All values in bytes/s.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_WANAccessSwitch string(22) RW 
Note: If the change is not possible the existing WANAccessType is retained.
Enumeration of:
- Ethernet (Try to switch WANAccessType to "Ethernet")
- FTTH_Switchover (Set value to "FTTH_Switchover" to try to swicth to fiber connection via ONT connected on the WAN/LAN1 port on dsl sync lost.)
- FTTH_Switchover_ReInit (Set value to "FTTH_Switchover_Reinit" to try to swicth to fiber connection via ONT connected on the WAN/LAN1 port on dsl sync lost with bootstrapping and reprovisioning on a new ACS connection)
- FTTH_Switchover_Off (Set value to "FTTH_Switchover_Off" to  reset a previously configured FTTH Switchover.)
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_WANAccessSwitchState string R 
After setting X_AVM-DE_WANAccessSwitch the status of switching could be determined with this parameter.
Successful change will result in: "PPPoE_success" or "TR069_success" (DHCP) for Ethernet settings or "FTTH_Switchover_pending" on FTTH_switchover
Otherwise an error is returned.
Enumeration of:
- None (No action has been started yet)
- Discovery_requested (Action has been started by the ACS)
- PPPoE_success (WAN access type changed successful by PPPoE discovery.)
- PPPoE_error_cable (No cable connected to WAN or LAN1)
- PPPoE_error_timeout (No PPPoE Server  found)
- PPPoE_error_dsl (DSL sync detected)
- PPPoE_error_internal (Socket or memory problem)
- TR069_success (WAN access type changed successful by TR069-DHCP discovery.)
- TR069_error_abort (DHCP client hangs on WAN or LAN1)
- TR069_error_down (During the tr069 discovery, the multid daemon has been terminated.)
- TR069_error_cable (No cable connected to WAN or LAN1)
- TR069_error_timeout (No DHCP Server  found)
- TR069_error_dsl (DSL sync detected)
- TR069_error_internal (Socket or memory problem)
- FTTH_Switchover_pending (A "FTTH_Switchover"  to switchover to a fiber connection via ONT connected on the WAN/LAN1 port on dsl sync lost is configured and pending.)
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_InternetConnectionLinkType string RW 
Returns the used Network Protocol "PPPoE" or "RBE" (Routed Bridged Ethernet). Setting this parameter with "PPPoE" or "RBE" will terminate the existing connection and establish a connection with the new linktype. Consider that if you switch from a RBE to a PPPoE connection Username/Password credentials are required for the PPP negotiation. If they dont't exist you must create a WANPPPConnection via AddObject and set Username/Password via SetParameterValue. This must be done beforehand or in the same TR-069 Session. If you don't do this the internet-dialin with PPPoE will fail because of missing credentials.
 - 7.70
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig. Object R 
only available on DSL-Boxes
 - 6.20
Enable boolean R
- 6.20
Status string R 
returns "Up"
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Initializing
- EstablishingLink
- NoSignal
- Error (OPTIONAL)
- Disabled
 - 6.20
LinkEncapsulationSupported string R 
Comma-separated list. Indicates which link encapsulation standards and recommendations are supported.
Enumeration of:
- G.992.3_Annex_K_ATM
- G.992.3_Annex_K_PTM
- G.993.2_Annex_K_ATM
- G.993.2_Annex_K_PTM
- G.994.1 (Auto)
 - 6.20
LinkEncapsulationRequested string RW 
availability depends on F!Box-hardware The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the LinkEncapsulationSupported parameter.
 - 6.20
LinkEncapsulationUsed string R 
availability depends on F!Box-hardware
Enumeration of:
- G.992.3_Annex_K_ATM
- G.992.3_Annex_K_PTM
- G.993.2_Annex_K_ATM
- G.993.2_Annex_K_PTM
 - 6.20
ModulationType string R 
DEPRECATED
Indicates the type of modulation used on the connection.
This parameter, which was inherited from WANDSLLinkConfig, is DEPRECATED because it is in general not clear which standards correspond to which of the the above enumerated values.
It is RECOMMENDED that the {{param|StandardUsed}} parameter be used to indicate which standard is in use.
Enumeration of:
- ADSL_G.dmt
- ADSL_G.lite
- ADSL_G.dmt.bis
- ADSL_re-adsl
- ADSL_2plus
- ADLS_four
- ADSL_ANSI_T1.413
- G.shdsl
- IDSL
- HDSL
- SDSL
- VDSL
 - 6.20
StandardUsed string R
- 6.20
StandardsSupported string R
- 6.50
CurrentProfile string R 
availability depends on F!Box-hardware
 - 6.20
UPBOKLE unsignedInt[0:1280] R 
availability depends on F!Box-hardware
 - 6.20
DataPath string R 
Enumeration of:
- Interleaved
- Fast
- None

availability depends on F!Box-hardware
 - 6.20

availability depends on F!Box-hardware

 - 6.20
ACTSNRMODEus unsignedInt R 
availability depends on F!Box-hardware
 - 6.20
ACTUALCE unsignedInt R

- 6.80
ATUCHECErrors unsignedInt R
- 6.80
CRCErrors unsignedInt R
- 6.80
ATUCCRCErrors unsignedInt R
- 6.80
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.X_AVM-DE_Negociated. Object R 
special DSL related negociated Parameters
 - 6.20
SRAus boolean R 
SRA upstream
 - 6.20
SRAds boolean R 
SRA downstream
 - 6.20
GVectus string R 
Vectoring upstream. Possible values are
"LEGACY",
"GVECT_FRIENDLY",
"GVECT_FULL"
 - 6.20
GVectds string R 
Vectoring downstream. Possible values are
"LEGACY",
"GVECT_FRIENDLY",
"GVECT_FULL".
 - 6.20
GInpus string R 
GInp upstream. Possible values are
"RTX_IN_USE", 
"RTX_NOT_IN_USE_FORBIDDEN",
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUC",
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUR", 
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUC_NOR_XTUR",
"NOT_IN_USE_REASON_UNDEFINED"
 - 6.20
GInpds string R 
GInp downstream. Possible values are
"RTX_IN_USE", 
"RTX_NOT_IN_USE_FORBIDDEN",
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUC",
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUR", 
"NOT_IN_USE_NOT_SUPPORTED_XTUC_NOR_XTUR",
"NOT_IN_USE_REASON_UNDEFINED"
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.DSLPerformance. Object R
- 6.80
OffsetDsMargin unsignedInt[0:4] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.
You can find the equivalent setting on "UI (Advanced view) - Internet - DSL Information - Line Settings - Intended signal-to-noise ratio (Receive Direction)".

Maximum Performance[0] ... Maximum Stability[4]
 - 6.80
DsINP unsignedInt[0:4] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.
You can find the equivalent setting on "UI (Advanced view) - Internet - DSL Information - Line Settings - Impulse Noise Protection (INP)".

Maximum Performance[0] ... Maximum Stability[4]
 - 6.80
RFI_mode unsignedInt[0:4] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.
You can find the equivalent setting on "UI (Advanced view) - Internet - DSL Information - Line Settings - Identification of radio frequency interference (RFI)".

Maximum Performance[0] ... Maximum Stability[4]
 - 6.80
UsNoiseBits unsignedInt[0:4] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.
You can find the equivalent setting on "UI (Advanced view) - Internet - DSL Information - Line Settings - Intended signal-to-noise ratio (Send Direction)".

Maximum Performance[0] ... Maximum Stability[4]
 - 6.80
DisableUs1WhenDs1LatnIsAboveDb unsignedInt[0:50] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.

Specifies the value of LATNds (band DS1) beyond which the band US1 is disabled.
'0' means that the default value of the DSL firmware is used, the maximum value is 50.
 0 7.00
DisableUs2WhenDs1LatnIsAboveDb unsignedInt[0:50] RW 
WARNING: Changing this value will result in a re-synchronization process of the dsl modem. Connection may be lost for a while.

Specifies the value of LATNds (band DS1) beyond which the band US2 is disabled.
'0' means that the default value of the DSL firmware is used, the maximum value is 50.
 0 7.00
LimitPHYToBookedRate boolean RW 
Limit DSL physical sync rate to booked sync rate.
Warning: Changing this value will result in a DSL resync.
 - 7.10

- 6.20
ATMCRCErrors unsignedInt R
- 6.20
ATMEncapsulation string RW 
Enumeration of:
- LLC
- VCMUX
 - 6.20
ATMPeakCellRate unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
ATMQoS string RW 
Enumeration of:
- UBR
- CBR
- GFR
- VBR-nrt
- VBR-rt
- UBR+
- ABR
 - 6.20
ATMReceivedBlocks unsignedInt R
- 6.20
ATMSustainableCellRate unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
ATMTransmittedBlocks unsignedInt R
- 6.20
AutoConfig boolean R
- 6.20
DestinationAddress string(256) RW
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW
false 6.20
LinkStatus string R 
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Down
- Initializing
- Unavailable
 - 6.20
LinkType string RW 
Enumeration of:
- EoA (RFC 2684 [RFC2684] bridged Ethernet over ATM)
- IPoA (RFC 2684 [RFC2684] routed IP over ATM)
- PPPoA (RFC 2364 [RFC2634] PPP over ATM)
- PPPoE (RFC 2516 [RFC2516] PPP over Ethernet on RFC 2684 [RFC2684] bridged Ethernet over ATM)
- CIP (RFC 2225 [RFC2225] Classical IP over ATM)
- Unconfigured
 Unconfigured 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_T-ONLINE-DE_LTE. Object R 
Only available for active provider "Telekom"
 - 7.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable or Disable the LTE modem
 - 7.20
Status string R 
The current operational state of the interface.
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Down
- Unknown
- Dormant
- NotPresent
- LowerLayerDown
- Error (OPTIONAL)
 - 7.20
Name string R 
The textual name of the interface as assigned by the UDV device.
Note: Always returns "lte0".
 - 7.20
AntennaMode string R 
"0": internal antenna, "1": external antenna
 - 7.20
Band string R 
Textual representation of the currently used band.

Notes:
 - The CPE will return "Extended GSM 900" when using the (deprecated) P-GSM 900 band.
 - The CPE will return an empty string when using a band other than those listed in the spec.
 - 7.20
CellID string R 
The eUTRAN CI of the cell where the LTE modem is registered.
 - 7.20
CurrentDownstreamRate string R 
Current downstream rate in kBit/s
 - 7.20
CurrentUpstreamRate string R 
Current upstream rate in kBit/s
 - 7.20
PLMN unsignedInt R 
The ID of the recognized operating agency (ROA) for mobile cell holding the connection.
 - 7.20
Roam string RW 
"Normal": No roaming
"Roam": Roam mode
Enumeration of:
- Roam
- Normal
 - 7.20
Roamstatus string R 
The current roam status.
Enumeration of:
- Roam
- Normal
 - 7.20
RSRP int R 
Unit: dBm. The signal strength value in dBm.
 - 7.20
RSRQ int R 
Unit: dBm. The Reference Signal Received Quality in dBm.
 - 7.20
RSSI int R 
Unit: dBm. RSSI of eNodeB in dBm
 - 7.20
ServiceDomain string R 
The network service do-main type.

Note: Always returns "PS_Capable".
Enumeration of:
- CS_Capable
- PS_Capable
- CS_PS_Capable
- NoService
 - 7.20
ServiceStatus string R 
The register network service type
Enumeration of:
- NoService
- LimitedService
- AvailableService
- LimitedRegionalService
- SleepService
 - 7.20
SignalQuality int R 
Strength of the receiving signal:
0 Nosignal
1 Veryweek
2 Weak
3 Normal
4 Strong
5 Very strong
9 other errors
 - 7.20
SignalStrengthPercent int R 
The signal strength as a percentage, adjusted to device capabilities
 - 7.20
SignalStrengthRaw int R 
Unspecified, currently equal to RSSI
 - 7.20
SysSubMode string R 
PS Mode
Enumeration of:
- NoService
- GSM
- GPRS
- EDGE
- WCDMA
- HSDPA
- LTE
 - 7.20
UpstreamMaxRate unsignedInt R 
Unit: kBit/s. Maximum Upstream Rate of the link mode in theory
 - 7.20
DownstreamMaxRate unsignedInt R 
Unit: kBit/s. Maximum Downstream Rate of the link mode in theory
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_T-ONLINE-DE_LTE.SIMCard. Object R

SIMStatus string R

- Invalid
- Valid
- CS_Invalid
- PS_Invalid
- PS_CS_Invalid
- NoCard
- PUKRequired
- PUKUnblockRequired
- SIMPermanentlyBlocked
- NoSignal
- Connecting
- NotInitialized
- Error
- PINEnabled

IMEI string R 
IMEI number
 - 7.20
PIN int RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 7.20
PINRetriesAvailable int R
- 7.20
PUK int RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 7.20
PUKRetriesAvailable int R
- 7.20
SIMICCID string R
- 7.20
SIMIMSI string R
- 7.20
MSISDN string R
- 7.20

APNname string RW

Password string RW

- DHCP
- Static

ConnectionStatus string R

- Disconneting
- Disconnecting

 OnDemand 6.20

- IP_Routed
- IP_Bridged

DefaultGateway string RW
- 6.20
DNSEnabled boolean RW
true 6.20

 false 6.20
DNSServers IPAddress(64) RW 
Comma-separated list.

X_AVM-DE_PreferedDNSServer IPAddress R 
IP Address of the system's prefered DNS server. Only available for "internet" connections.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean R
false 6.20
ExternalIPAddress IPAddress RW
- 6.20
LastConnectionError string R 
Enumeration of:
- ERROR_NONE

- ERROR_USER_DISCONNECT
- ERROR_ISP_DISCONNECT
- ERROR_IDLE_DISCONNECT
- ERROR_FORCED_DISCONNECT

 ERROR_NONE 6.20
MACAddress MACAddress RW

Name string(256) RW
- 6.20
NATEnabled boolean RW

PortMappingNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
PossibleConnectionTypes string R 
Comma-separated list.
Enumeration of:
- Unconfigured
- IP_Routed
- IP_Bridged
 - 6.20
Reset boolean RW
false 6.20
RouteProtocolRx string RW 
support "Off" and "RIPv2"
Enumeration of:
- Off
- RIPv1 (OPTIONAL)
- RIPv2 (OPTIONAL)
- OSPF (OPTIONAL)
 Off 6.20
RSIPAvailable boolean R
- 6.20
SubnetMask IPAddress RW
- 6.20
Uptime unsignedInt R
- 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv4_Proxy_Custom_Forward string RW 
Commaseparated list of DNSv4 servers. They will be used instead of "DNSServers" for DNS requests from connected LAN clients (Homenet).
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv4_Proxy_Custom_Forward_Enable boolean RW 
If true, override DNSv4 server assigned by DHCP.
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv6_Proxy_Custom_Forward string RW 
Commaseparated list of DNSv6 servers. They will be used instead of "DNSServers" for DNS requests from connected LAN clients (Homenet).
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv6_Proxy_Custom_Forward_Enable boolean RW 
If true, override DNSv6 server assigned by DHCPv6 or autoconfiguration..
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_DHCP_ClassID string(128) RW 
Vendor specific DHCP class identifier
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_DHCP_ClientID string(255) RW 
Client ID (Option 61) for WAN DHCP.
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_reconnect_delay unsignedInt RW 
Waiting time in seconds until the connection is reestablished after disconnect. Allowed values 0 ... 3600.
 0 7.50
X_AVM-DE_Filter_SIP boolean RW 
Filter SIP traffic from LAN.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Filter_SMTP boolean RW 
Filter SMTP traffic from LAN.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_SIP_ALG_Enabled boolean RW 
If true, SIP_ALG-Feature is enabled
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_SIP_MasqTime unsignedInt[0:3600] RW 
Step size is 0. Unit: seconds. Time for an SIP Masquerading entry
 300 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Stackmode string RW 
Assign IP protocol (IPv4, IPv6, Dualstack) to be used on this connection.
Special behaviour on 'internet' connection: IP protocol on internet connection is defined by object 'InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.' Read access will always return "n/a". Write access will be ignored.
Allowed values: "ipv4only", "ipv6only", "dual"
 - 6.80
X_AVM-DE_ARP_reply_for_any_local boolean RW 
Flag for internet connections via DHCP. If this parameter is true FRITZ!OS will reply ARP requests coming from a sender outside their assigned IPv4 subnet.
 false 7.20

InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANIPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHost. Object R

Please use the InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WAN*Connection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHosts.{i}. table to configure exposed hosts.
 - 6.50
Enabled boolean RW
- 6.50
IPAddress string RW 
The destination host
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANIPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHosts.{i}. Object R 
Specifies a complete port forwarding (all ports)
WARNING: After configuring exposed hosts via this table, the object InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WAN*Connection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHost. MUST NOT be written to.
Note that previously created exposed host configurations will also appear in this table.
 - 7.20
Enable boolean RW
- 7.20
IPAddress string RW 
The destination host
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANIPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_PublicIPSubnet.{i}. Object R 
There are 3 predefined PublicIPSubnet Objects.
 - 7.50
Enable boolean RW

 - 7.00
Subnet string RW 
Subnet prefix
 - 7.00
SubnetMask string RW
- 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANIPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Onlinecheck. Object R 
DNS based failure detection.
 - 8.10
Offlinemode unsignedInt[0:2] RW 
DNS based failure detection. Allowed values for Offlinemode are:
0: no DNS checks are performed
1: check always: If no DNS server responds, the status goes to “offline”
2: auto: Only if at least one of the configured DNS servers has given an answer once, will go into 'offline' status.
 0 8.10
Checkmode unsignedInt[0:1] RW 
Checkmode. Allowed values are:
0: interval
1: dead peer detection
 1 8.10
Checkinterval unsignedInt RW 
Checkinterval in seconds. Only applicable if Checkmode  is "0: interval".
 60 8.10
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}. Object RW

Username string(64) RW
6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv4_Proxy_Custom_Forward string RW 
Commaseparated list of DNSv4 servers. They will be used instead of "DNSServers" for DNS requests from connected LAN clients (Homenet).
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv4_Proxy_Custom_Forward_Enable boolean RW 
If true, override DNSv4 server assigned by PPP.
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv6_Proxy_Custom_Forward string RW 
Commaseparated list of DNSv6 servers. They will be used instead of "DNSServers" for DNS requests from connected LAN clients (Homenet).
 - 6.20
X_AVM_DE_DNSv6_Proxy_Custom_Forward_Enable boolean RW 
If true, override DNSv6 server assigned by DHCPv6 or autoconfiguration..
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_DisconnectPreventionEnable boolean RW 
Forced disconnect
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_DisconnectPreventionHour unsignedInt RW 
Forced disconnect time
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Filter_SIP boolean RW 
Filter SIP traffic from LAN.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Filter_SMTP boolean RW 
Filter SMTP traffic from LAN.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_LCPEcho_Prio int RW 
VLAN Prio (P-Bit) of LCP Echo Packets according 802.1p.  Allowed Values 0...7.
All WANPPPConnections use the same VLAN prio for LCP Echo packets.
 - 6.80
X_AVM-DE_PPPoEPassthroughEnable boolean RW 
Enable PPPoE passthrough
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_SIP_ALG_Enabled boolean RW 
If true, SIP_ALG-Feature is enabled
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_SIP_MasqTime unsignedInt[0:3600] RW 
Step size is 0. Unit: seconds. Time for an SIP Masquerading entry
 300 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Stackmode string RW 
Assign IP protocol (IPv4, IPv6, Dualstack) to be used on this connection.
Special behaviour on 'internet' connection: IP protocol on internet connection is defined by object 'InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.' Read access will always return "n/a". Write access will be ignored.
Allowed values: "ipv4only", "ipv6only", "dual"
 - 6.80
X_AVM-DE_Username_Prefix_List string(31) RW 
Comma-separated list. The Realm extensions are used to extend the username (front) during a pppoe connection process.
Realms are used from left to right. 
A valid realm will be moved to the front - it will be used automatically in the next connection process.

Examples:
",W"     -> Empty entry at the beginning
"H,,X"   -> Empty entry at the middle
"A,B,"   -> Empty entry at the end
"A"       -> Just one single entry
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Username_Prefix_VDSL_List string(31) RW 
Comma-separated list. The Realm extensions are used to extend the username (front) during a pppoe connection process.
Realms are used from left to right. 
A valid realm will be moved to the front - it will be used automatically in the next connection process.

Examples:
",W"     -> Empty entry at the beginning
"H,,X"   -> Empty entry at the middle
"A,B,"   -> Empty entry at the end
"A"       -> Just one single entry
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_RFC4638 boolean RW 
Enables a MTU/MRU greater than 1492 in the PPPoE protocol. See RFC4638.
 false 7.00
X_AVM-DE_MTU unsignedInt RW 
MTU-Size. 0 means default.
 0 7.90
X_AVM-DE_reconnect_delay unsignedInt RW 
Waiting time in seconds until the connection is reestablished after disconnect. Allowed values 0 ... 3600.
 0 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.PortMapping.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: RemoteHost, ExternalPort, PortMappingProtocol.
 - 6.20
PortMappingEnabled boolean RW
false 6.20
PortMappingLeaseDuration unsignedInt RW 
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
 - 6.20
RemoteHost string RW
6.20
ExternalPort unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
ExternalPortEndRange unsignedInt RW 
restriction: only for connection 'internet'
 0 6.20
InternalPort unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
PortMappingProtocol string RW 
Enumeration of:
- TCP
- UDP
 - 6.20
InternalClient string(256) RW 
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
 6.20
PortMappingDescription string(256) RW
6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.Stats. Object R
- 6.20
EthernetBytesSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetBytesReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetErrorsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetErrorsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetUnicastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetUnicastPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetDiscardPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetDiscardPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetMulticastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetMulticastPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetBroadcastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetBroadcastPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
EthernetUnknownProtoPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHost. Object R 
Specifies a complete port forwarding (all ports)
Please use the InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WAN*Connection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHosts.{i}. table to configure exposed hosts.
 - 6.50
Enabled boolean RW
- 6.50
IPAddress string RW 
The destination host
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHosts.{i}. Object R 
Specifies a complete port forwarding (all ports)

WARNING: After configuring exposed hosts via this table, the object InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WAN*Connection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ExposedHost. MUST NOT be written to.
Note that previously created exposed host configurations will also appear in this table.
 - 7.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enables or disables this exposed host instance
 - 7.20
IPAddress string(16) RW 
Local IP address of the exposed host
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_PublicIPSubnet.{i}. Object R 
There are 3 predefined PublicIPSubnet Objects.
 - 7.50
Enable boolean RW 
Enabled/Disables public subnet.
 - 7.00
Subnet string RW 
Subnet prefix
 - 7.00
SubnetMask string RW
- 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANPPPConnection.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Onlinecheck. Object R 
DNS based failure detection.
 - 8.10
Offlinemode unsignedInt[0:2] RW 
DNS based failure detection. Allowed values for Offlinemode are:
0: no DNS checks are performed
1: check always: If no DNS server responds, the status goes to “offline”
2: auto: Only if at least one of the configured DNS servers has given an answer once, will go into 'offline' status.
 0 8.10
Checkmode unsignedInt[0:1] RW 
Step size is 0. Checkmode. Allowed values are:
0: interval
1: dead peer detection
 1 8.10
Checkinterval unsignedInt RW 
Checkinterval in seconds. Only applicable if Checkmode  is "0: interval".
 60 8.10
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_WANLTELinkConfig. Object R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware
 - 6.20
APN string RW
- 7.00
Uptime unsignedInt R
- 7.00
APN_VoIP string RW 
Name of the Voice APN. Only applicable if "EnableVoIPPDN" is true.
 - 6.20
CellId string R 
Retrieves the ID of the primary cell.
 - 6.20
CellInfoNumberOfEntries int R 
Value is always 10.
 - 7.00
Distance int R 
Retrieves the approximate distance (in meters) to the NodeB of the primary cell.
 - 7.00
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
EnableVoIPPDN boolean RW 
Activates or resets a separate VoIP connection.
 - 6.20
IMSI string R
- 6.20
PhysicalId int R 
Retrieves the physical ID of the primary cell.
 - 7.00
PLMN string R
- 6.20
PPPPassword string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. PPP Password of internet connection.
 - 6.50
PPPPasswordVoIP string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. PPP Password of VoIP connection.
 - 6.50
PPPUsername string(64) RW 
PPP Username of internet connection.
 - 6.50
PPPUsernameVoIP string(64) RW 
PPPUsername of VoIP connection.
 - 6.50
PPPAuthProtocol int RW 
Used PPP Authentication Protocol for the internet connection. Supported values: 1=PAP, 2=CHAP
 - 7.55
PPPAuthProtocolVoIP int RW 
Used PPP Authentication Protocol for the VoIP connection. Supported values: 1=PAP, 2=CHAP
 - 7.55
Provider string R 
Retrieves the PLMN's name as a human-readable string.
 - 7.00
RSRP int R 
Retrieves the RSRP of the primary cell (in dBm, range -140 to 0).
 - 7.00
RSRQ int R 
Retrieves the RSRP of the primary cell (in dBm, range -140 to 0).
 - 7.50
ICCID string R 
ICCID is the unique identifier of each SIM card
 - 8.10
SerialNumber string R 
Retrieves the serial number/IMEI of the WWAN modem.
 - 7.00
SimRetryCount unsignedInt R 
Retrieves the number of SIM PIN tries that are still allowed.
 - 7.00
SoftwareVersion string R 
Retrieves the software version of the WWAN modem.
 - 7.00
PDN1_MTU unsignedInt RW 
Defines MTU size of the first PDN. If 0 use default.
 0 7.20
PDN2_MTU unsignedInt RW 
Defines MTU size of the second PDN if exists. If 0 use default.
 0 7.20
TAC string R 
Retrieves the tracking area code.
 - 7.00
Roaming boolean RW 
Allow or disallow roaming.
 - 7.20
CurrentAccessTechnology string R 
Returns the currently used mobile Access Technolgy. Enumeration of: "GSM", "EDGE", "HSDPA", "LTE", "LTE-5GCN"", "5G-SA", "5G-NSA", "unknown"
 - 7.50
EnabledAccessTechnologies string RW 
Comma-separated list (0:5). Returns and sets the enabled mobile Access Technologies as comma separated List or auto for default.If the list contains technologies which are not supported by the modem the request gets rejected.
Enumeration of:
- GSM
- UMTS
- LTE
- 5G-NSA
- 5G-SA
- AUTO
 auto 7.50
PreferredAccessTechnology string(64) RW 
Returns and sets the preferred mobile Access Technologies or auto for default. The box will try to connect via the preferred technology first. Other rats will be used as fallback. 
Only available if 5G is supported
Enumeration of:
- NR5G
- LTE
- WCDMA
 auto 7.50
AccessTechnologyCapabilities string R 
Returns the mobile Access Technolgies the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. E.g. "UMTS,LTE,5G-NSA,5G-SA"
 - 7.50
BandCapabilitiesLTE string R 
Returns LTE bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if LTE is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilitiesLTE string(64) RW 
Returns LTE bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if LTE is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilities5GNSA string(64) RW 
Returns 5G-NSA bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilities5GSA string(64) RW 
Returns 5G-SA bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
BandCapabilities5GNSA string R 
Returns 5G-NSA bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported.
 - 7.50
BandCapabilities5GSA string R 
Returns 5G-SA bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
SignalRSRP0 string R 
Returns the RSRP-Values of the PCC-Cell connection for each antenna. The values are coded als key=value-pairs. Each rxN kvp represents one antenna so a CPE with four antennas will have rx1-rx4 pairs. The order may change over time. Values can be empty as 'rx1=' if no value was given. The 'main' kvp provides an average over all antenna values. On some CPE 'rxNtxN' kvps are available. These provide the PCC-Cell Antenna RSRP for the given CPE antenna. If in question contact AVM.
 - 7.50
SignalRSRP1 string R 
Returns the RSRP-Values of the SCC-Cell connection. Returns the RSRP-Values of the SCC-Cell connection foreach antenna
What is considered as SCC depends on the used  CPE RAT. For 5G-NSA the SCC is the NSA cell.

The values are coded als key=value-pairs. Each rxN kvp represents one antenna so a CPE with four antenna will have rx1-rx4 pairs. The order may change over time. Values can be empty as 'rx1=' if no value was given. The 'main' kvp provide an average over all antenna values. On some CPE 'rxNtxN' kvps are available.
These provide the Cell-Antenna RSRP for the given CPE antenna. 

If in question contact AVM.
 - 7.50
ConnectedCellsIndexList string R 
Returns list of cell-Index-values that represent the connected cells in the CellInfoTable Entries as comma separated List. Please be aware that the index represent not the table index but matches the value in the CellIndex Parameter in each CellInfo Entry
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_WANLTELinkConfig.CellInfo.{i}. Object R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware.
 - 7.00
CellVisible boolean R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware. If 0, the remaining parameters of this object will not contain sensible data.
 - 7.00
PhysicalId string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware.
 - 7.50
CellRssi string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware. Retrieves the RSSI  of the NodeB (in dBm) as up to 5 CSV.
 - 7.00
CellRsrq string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware. Retrieves the RSRQ (in dBm, range -64 to 0) as up to 5 CSV.
 - 7.00
CellRsrp string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware. Retrieves the RSRP (in dBm). Format: (Reported, Rx0Tx0, Rx0Tx1, Rx1Tx0, Rx1Tx1, Max-Rx0, Max-Rx1, potentially Rx0Tx2, Rx0Tx3, Rx1Tx2, Rx1Tx3)
 - 7.00
CellId string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware.
 - 7.00
CellIndex int R 
Shows the index in the celllist. May be referenced by X_AVM-DE_WANLTELinkConfig.ConnectedCellsIndexList.
 - 7.50
CellType string R 
Shows the Celltype
Enumeration of:
- gsm
- umts
- lte
- nr5g
 - 7.50
CellConnected string R 
Shows the current
Enumeration of:
- PCC
- SCC
- neighbour
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_WANUMTSLinkConfig. Object R 
Only available on boxes supporting internet connection via connected USB Mobile  stick.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable mobile internet access.
 - 6.20
CellId string R 
Mobile CellId
 - 6.20
APN string RW 
APN Name
 - 7.50
IMSI string R
- 6.20
IMEI string R
- 6.20
Manufacturer string R 
Manufacturer of used mobile stick.
 - 6.20
Model string R 
Model of used mobile stick.
 - 6.20
FirmwareVersion string R 
Firmwareversion of used mobile stick.
 - 6.20
MobileTelephonyMSN string RW 
Telephony MSN of the Mobile Stick.
 - 7.20
MobileTelephonyDisplayname string RW 
MobileTelephony name shown in the display.
 - 7.20
PPPUsername string RW 
PPP Username of internet connection for mobile connection via USB mobile stick.
 - 7.50
PPPPassword string RW 
PPP Password of internet connection for mobile connection via USB mobile stick. Reading this parameter returns a empty string.
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_WANUMTSLinkConfig.Backup. Object R 
Fallback functionality.  In case of  loss of the primary internet connection a internet connection via mobile stick will be established.
 - 6.20
Enabled boolean RW 
Enable mobile  backup
 - 6.20
AdvancedFailureBackup boolean RW 
Enables advanced failover that triggers on all types of errors on the primary connection (PPP error, ...) instead of only sync loss.
Requires a working mobile backup device (USB Serial or MBIM).
On FRITZ!Box 6890, this parameter is a no-op.
 - 7.20
Quickstart unsignedInt R 
Only available for providers with UMTS-Quickstart functionality. Returned values:
0: quickstart_off
1: quickstart_active
2: quickstart_done
 - 6.20
Downtime unsignedInt[0:86400] RW 
Unit: seconds. Time after connection loss switching over to mobile connection.
 180 6.20
Reverttime unsignedInt[0:86400] RW 
Unit: seconds. Time switching from mobile connection to primary connection after regaining a stable primary connection.
 1800 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_ARCOR_NET_LTEModem. Object R 
Only available for active provider "Vodafone"
 - 6.20
CellId string R 
Retrieves the ID of the primary cell.
 - 6.20
PreferredAccessTechnology string(64) RW 
Returns and sets the preferred mobile Access Technologies or auto for default. The box will try to connect via the preferred technology first. Other rats will be used as fallback. 
Only available if 5G is supported
Enumeration of:
- NR5G
- LTE
- WCDMA
 auto 7.50
ConnectedCellsIndexList string R 
Returns list of cell-Index-values that represent the connected cells in the CellInfoTable Entries as comma separated List. Please be aware that the index represent not the table index but matches the value in the CellIndex Parameter in each CellInfo Entry
 - 7.50
SignalRSRP1 string R 
Returns the RSRP-Values of the SCC-Cell connection. Returns the RSRP-Values of the SCC-Cell connection foreach antenna
What is considered as SCC depends on the used  CPE RAT. For 5G-NSA the SCC is the NSA cell.

The values are coded als key=value-pairs. Each rxN kvp represents one antenna so a CPE with four antenna will have rx1-rx4 pairs. The order may change over time. Values can be empty as 'rx1=' if no value was given. The 'main' kvp provide an average over all antenna values. On some CPE 'rxNtxN' kvps are available.
These provide the Cell-Antenna RSRP for the given CPE antenna. 

If in question contact AVM.
 - 7.50
SignalRSRP0 string R 
Returns the RSRP-Values of the PCC-Cell connection for each antenna. The values are coded als key=value-pairs. Each rxN kvp represents one antenna so a CPE with four antennas will have rx1-rx4 pairs. The order may change over time. Values can be empty as 'rx1=' if no value was given. The 'main' kvp provides an average over all antenna values. On some CPE 'rxNtxN' kvps are available. These provide the PCC-Cell Antenna RSRP for the given CPE antenna. If in question contact AVM.
 - 7.50
BandCapabilities5GSA string R 
Returns 5G-SA bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
BandCapabilities5GNSA string R 
Returns 5G-NSA bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilities5GSA string(64) RW 
Returns 5G-SA bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilities5GNSA string(64) RW 
Returns 5G-NSA bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if 5G is supported
 - 7.50
EnabledBandCapabilitiesLTE string(64) RW 
Returns LTE bands that are enabled on the CPE of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if LTE is supported
 - 7.50
BandCapabilitiesLTE string R 
Returns LTE bands the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. Values are provided as numbers without the 'n'-prefix e.g. "1,3,5..."
Only available if LTE is supported
 - 7.50
CellInfoNumberOfEntries int R 
Value is always 10.
 - 7.00
Distance int R 
Unit: m. Retrieves the approximate distance  to the NodeB of the primary cell.
 - 7.00
Enable boolean RW 
Returns true if the LTE modem is enabled.
 - 6.20
EnableVoIPPDN boolean RW 
Activates or resets a separate VoIP connection.
 - 6.20
PDN1_MTU unsignedInt[0:4096] RW 
Step size is 0. Defines MTU size of the first PDN. If 0 use default.
 0 7.20
PDN2_MTU unsignedInt[0:4096] RW 
Step size is 0. Defines MTU size of the second PDN if exists. If 0 use default.
 0 7.20
PhysicalId int R 
Retrieves the physical ID of the primary cell.
 - 7.00
PLMN string R 
Provider ID
 - 6.20
PPPPassword string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. PPP Password of internet connection.
 - 6.50
PPPPasswordVoIP string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. PPP Password of VoIP connection.
 - 6.50
PPPUsername string(64) RW 
PPP Username of internet connection.
 - 6.50
PPPUsernameVoIP string(64) RW 
PPP Username of VoIP connection.
 - 6.50
Provider string R 
Retrieves the PLMN's name as a human-readable string.
 - 7.00
Roaming boolean RW 
Allow or disallow roaming.
 - 7.20
RSRP int[-140:0] R 
Step size is 0. Unit: dBm. Retrieves the RSRP of the primary cell
 - 7.00
SerialNumber string R 
Retrieves the serial number/IMEI of the WWAN modem.
 - 7.00
SimRetryCount unsignedInt R 
Retrieves the number of SIM PIN tries that are still allowed.
 - 7.00
SoftwareVersion string R 
Retrieves the software version of the WWAN modem.
 - 7.00
TAC string R 
Retrieves the tracking area code.
 - 7.00
Uptime unsignedInt R 
Unit: s.
 - 7.00
SimIMSI string R 
Retrieves the IMSI of the SIM card.
 - 6.80
RSRQ int R 
Retrieves the RSRP of the primary cell (in dBm, range -140 to 0).
 - 7.00
VoiceAPNName string(64) RW 
Gets or sets the voice APN name
 - 6.80
CurrentAccessTechnology string R 
Returns the currently used mobile Access Technolgy. Enumeration of: "GSM", "EDGE", "HSDPA", "LTE", "LTE-5GCN"", "5G-SA", "5G-NSA", "unknown"
 - 7.50
EnabledAccessTechnologies string RW 
Returns and sets the enabled mobile Access Technologies as comma separated List or auto for default. E.g. "auto" or "LTE,5G-NSA" or "UMTS,LTE,5G-NSA,5G-SA"... .
If the list contains technologies which are not supported by the modem the request gets rejected.
 auto 7.50
AccessTechnologyCapabilities string R 
Returns the mobile Access Technolgies the CPE is capable of as comma separated List. E.g. "UMTS,LTE,5G-NSA,5G-SA"
 - 7.50
DataAPNName string(64) RW 
Gets or sets the data APN name
 - 6.80
NetworkStatus string R 
Enumeration of:
- 1 (Only data APN registered)
- 5 (Voice and data APN registered)
 - 6.80
VoiceRSRPThreshold int RW 
Unit: dBm. Always returns -105.
Write accesses have no effect.
 - 6.80
Manufacturer string R 
Always returns "AVM"
 - 6.80
ModelName string R 
Returns the human-readable CPE name, e.g. "FRITZ!Box 6890 LTE"
 - 6.80
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_ARCOR_NET_LTEModem.CellInfo.{i}. Object R 
Only available for active provider "Vodafone"
 - 6.20
CellVisible boolean R 
Only available on LTE Hardware. If 0, the remaining parameters of this object will not contain sensible data.
 - 6.20
CellIndex int R 
Shows the index in the celllist. May be referenced by X_ARCOR_NET_LTE_Modem.ConnectedCellsIndexList.
 - 7.50
CellType string R 
Shows the Celltype
Enumeration of:
- gsm
- umts
- lte
- nr5g
 - 7.50
CellConnected string R 
Shows the current
Enumeration of:
- PCC
- SCC
- neighbour
 - 7.50
PhysicalId string R 
Only available on LTE-Hardware.
 - 7.50
CellRssi string R 
Comma-separated list (0:5). Only available on LTE Hardware. Retrieves the RSSI  of the NodeB (in dBm) as up to 5 CSV.
 - 6.20
CellRsrq string R 
Comma-separated list (0:5). Only available on LTE Hardware. Retrieves the RSRQ (in dBm, range -64 to 0) as up to 5 CSV.
 - 6.20
CellRsrp string R 
Comma-separated list (1:11). Only available on LTE Hardware. Retrieves the RSRP (in dBm). Format: (Reported, Rx0Tx0, Rx0Tx1, Rx1Tx0, Rx1Tx1, Max-Rx0, Max-Rx1, potentially Rx0Tx2, Rx0Tx3, Rx1Tx2, Rx1Tx3)
 - 6.20
CellId string R 
Only available on LTE Hardware.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Optical. Object R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Optical.Interface. Object R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Optical.Interface.{i}. Object R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
OpticalSignalLevel int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
AutodetectMode boolean RW 
Enables or disables the GPON/AON mode change autodetect for all supporting devices (currently only 56X0). If successfully enabled, the device will react to an optical signal that does not match the current mode (GPON or AON) by switching to the other respective mode.
 - 8.10
LowerOpticalThreshold int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
UpperOpticalThreshold int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
TransmitOpticalLevel int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
LowerTransmitPowerThreshold int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
UpperTransmitPowerThreshold int R 
Unit: dBm/1000. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
SFPVendor string R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.20
SFPPartNumber string R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.25
SFPSerialNumber string R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.25
SFPType int R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
Types:
0 = Unknown
1 = GPON
2 = XGPON
3 = XGSPON
4 = 1000BASE_BX10 (1G AON)
5 = 10GBASE_BX (10G AON)
6 = 1000BASE_T
 - 7.25
TXWavelength unsignedInt R 
Unit: nm. Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.20
GponSerial string R 
Serial Number of this device
 - 7.55
PONId string R 
Identifier of the OLT PON Interface, e.g. to reflect the established logical address plan
 - 7.55
ONUId unsignedInt R 
Optical Network Unit Identifier, assigned by OLT during activation
 - 7.55
UNIType string R 
Type of User Network Interface,  possible values are "PPTP" or "VEIP"
Enumeration of:
- PPTP
- VEIP
 - 7.55
GEMPortCount unsignedInt R 
Number of GEM Ports (Management/Traffic Ports) provisioned by the OLT
 - 7.55
FiberMode string R 
The current Fiber Mode: possible values are "GPON", "XGPON", "XGSPON", "1000BASE_BX10", "10GBASE_BX", "1000BASE_T"
Enumeration of:
- GPON
- XGPON
- XGSPON
- 1000BASE_BX10
- 10GBASE_BX
- 1000BASE_T
 - 7.55
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Optical.Interface.{i}.Stats. Object R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_Optical.Interface.{i}.Stats.Day. Object R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
BytesSent unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
BytesReceived unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
PacketsSent unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
PacketsReceived unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
PacketErrorsSent unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
PacketErrorsReceived unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
PacketsMulticast unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.55
ConnectionRateDown unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00
ConnectionRateUp unsignedInt R 
Only available on fiber hardware.
 - 7.00

InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_DOCSIS. Object R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
Standard string R 
Returns a semicolon-separated list of the DOCSIS operational modes used by this modem. This list always contains 2 entries, of which either one may be empty.
Examples:
 - "DOCSIS 2.0;"
 - "DOCSIS 3.0;DOCSIS 3.1"
 - ";DOCSIS 3.1"
 - 7.00
NumDownstreamTuners unsignedInt R 
Returns the number of downstream tuners (usually 1).
 - 7.00
NumDownstreamReceivers unsignedInt R 
Returns the number of downstream receivers (e.g. 24 for the F!Box 6490)
 - 7.00
NumUpstreamChannels unsignedInt R 
Returns the number of upstream channels (e.g. 4 for the F!Box 6430).
 - 7.00
MACInitReason string R 
Returns the MAC init reason (usually "Power On")
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-2-1-1-1 string R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.1 (sysDescr) value
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-4-1-4491-2-1-28-1-18-2 int R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.4.1.4491.2.1.28.1.18.2 (docsIf31CmSystemCfgStateDiplexerCfgBandEdge) value
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-4-1-4491-2-1-28-1-18-4 int R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.4.1.4491.2.1.28.1.18.4 (docsIf31CmSystemCfgStateDiplexerCfgDsLowerBandEdge) value
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-4-1-4491-2-1-28-1-18-6 int R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.4.1.4491.2.1.28.1.18.6 (docsIf31CmSystemCfgStateDiplexerCfgDsUpperBandEdge) value
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-2-1-69-1-5-8-1-7 string R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.2.1.69.1.5.8.1.7 (docsDevEvText) value
 - 7.50
OID-1-3-6-1-2-1-10-127-1-2-1-1-1-2 string R 
Returns SNMP node 1.3.6.1.2.1.10.127.1.2.1.1.1.2 (docsIfCmCmtsAddress.2) value
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_DOCSIS.Downstream.{i}. Object R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
Frequency unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In Hz.
 - 7.00
Modulation string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. Retrieves the modulation used by this downstream receiver.
Enumeration of:
- 4QAM (AKA QPSK.)
- 8QAM
- 16QAM
- 32QAM
- 64QAM
- 128QAM
- 256QAM
- 1024QAM
- 2048QAM (DOCSIS 3.1 and later only.)
- 4096QAM (DOCSIS 3.1 and later only.)
- ??? (Generic error value.)
 - 7.00
MSE string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. Retrieves the Mean Squared Error [dB] as a signed real number.
 - 7.00
QAMLock boolean R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
FECLock boolean R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
MPEGLock boolean R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
AnnexType string R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
Enumeration of:
- A
- B
 - 7.00
ReceivedWords unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. Returns  the total number of received code words.
 - 7.00
CorrectedWords unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. Returns  the number of corrupted-but-recoverable code words received.
 - 7.00
UncorrectedWords unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. Returns  the number of unrecoverably corrupted code words received.
 - 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.X_AVM-DE_DOCSIS.Upstream.{i}. Object R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
Frequency unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In Hz.
 - 7.00
SymbolRate unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In Hz.
 - 7.00
MultiplexMethod string R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
Enumeration of:
- ATDMA
- SCDMA
 - 7.00
Attenuation string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dB.
 - 7.00
ReportedPower string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dBmV.
 - 7.00
PHigh string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dBmV.
 - 7.00
PLow string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dBmV.
 - 7.00
PDRWHigh string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dBmV. DRW = Dynamic Range Window.
 - 7.00
PDRWLow string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dBmV. DRW = Dynamic Range Window.
 - 7.00
DeltaPF string R 
Only available on Cable hardware. In dB.
 - 7.00
Modulation string R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
Enumeration of:
- 4QAM (AKA QPSK.)
- 8QAM
- 16QAM
- 32QAM
- 64QAM
- 128QAM
- 256QAM
- 1024QAM
- 2048QAM (DOCSIS 3.1 and later only.)
- 4096QAM (DOCSIS 3.1 and later only.)
- ??? (Generic error value.)
 - 7.00
SID unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
UCID unsignedInt R 
Only available on Cable hardware.
 - 7.00
InternetGatewayDevice.User.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Username. see TR-157. User credentials for remote access One (1) object is preinstanciated. Only one (1) user object supported.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
false 6.20
X_AVM-DE_2FA_off boolean RW 
Disable two-factor authentication for this remote access user.
 false 7.50
RemoteAccessCapable boolean RW
false 6.20
Username string(64) RW 
Note Security Issue:
Write Access to "Username" triggers internally the creation of a random generated username. The random generated username can be read by the ACS with GetParameterValue. This mechanism prevents that a ACS configures the remote access of all CPEs with the same user credentials.
 6.20
Password string(8:64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 6.20
Language string(16) RW 
Language switch not supported
 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.UPnP. Object R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.UPnP.Device. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable/disable UPnP
 - 6.20
UPnPMediaServer boolean RW 
Enable/disable MediaServer
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}. Object R 
IPv6 Settings
 - 6.20
DNS1 string R 
IPv6 address of first DNS Server
 - 6.20
PortMappingNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 7.10
DNS1_Origin string R 
Delivers the origin of the DNS1 IPv6 address.  Possible values: "static", "ra", "dhcp6", "".
 - 6.20
DNS2 string R 
IPv6 address of second DNS Server
 - 6.20
DNS2_Origin string R 
Delivers the origin of the DNS2 IPv6 address.  Possible values: "static", "ra", "dhcp6", "".
 - 6.20
DP string R 
Delegated Prefix.
 - 6.20
DP_PreferedLifetime unsignedInt R 
Prefered lifetime of delegated prefix in seconds.
Value = -1 indicatices INFINITE.
 - 6.20
DP_ValidLifetime unsignedInt R 
Valid lifetime of delegated prefix in seconds.
Value = -1 indicatices INFINITE.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
IPAddress string R 
Connection IPv6 address including prefix.
 - 6.20
LANPrefixLen unsignedInt[48:63] RW 
The length of the LAN Prefix.
 - 6.20
Manual_AFTR_FQDN string RW 
OEM specific parameter. Not available on all F!Box models.
 - 6.20
Mode string RW 
IPv6 Mode to obtain the global IPv6 address of the router.
Following modes are supprted (Strings are case sensitive!):
"ipv6_off"
"ipv6_ipv4native" (native IPv4 und native IPv6, Dual Stack)
"ipv6_tunnel_6to4"
"ipv6_tunnel_6rd"
"ipv6_tunnel_6in4"
"ipv6_tunnel_sixxs"
"ipv6_native" (auto negociate  global IPv6 address)
"ipv6_native_dhcp6" (get global address only by DHCPv6)
"ipv6_native_prefix" (get global address by prefix assignment)
"ipv6_native_static" (use static configuration)
 - 6.20
MTU unsignedInt RW 
Write access with values >= 1280 will override the default MTU. Value=0 will switch to default MTU.
 - 6.20
NTP1 string R 
IPv6 address of first NTP Server
 - 6.20
NTP1_Origin string R 
Delivers the origin of the NTP1 IPv6 address.  Possible values: "static", "ra", "dhcp6", "".
 - 6.20
NTP2 string R 
IPv6 address of second NTP Server
 - 6.20
NTP2_Origin string R 
Delivers the origin of the NTP2 IPv6 address.  Possible values: "static", "ra", "dhcp6", "".
 - 6.20
Origin string R 
Delivers the origin of the global IPv6 address.  Possible values: "ra", "ia_na", "ia_pd", "6to4", "6rd".
 - 6.20
PreferedLifetime string R 
eUint. Connection prefered lifetime in seconds. 
Value = -1 indicatices INFINITE.
Missspelling of Preferred but kept because working
 - 6.20
RequestLANPrefixLen boolean RW 
Request a specific LAN Prefix length by setting this paramter to true. The requested LAN Prefix length is specified by LANPrefixLen.
 - 6.20
Status string R 
Returns the current state:  "disabled", "disconnected",  "connecting", "connected"
 - 6.20
Uptime string R 
eUint. Connection uptime in sec.
 - 6.20
ValidLifetime string R 
eUint. Connection valid lifetime in seconds. 
Value = -1 indicatices INFINITE.
 - 6.20
WANConnectionDevice string RW 
Indicates reference of this object to the wan connection. Currently only "..WANConnection.1" is supported.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.PortMapping.{i}. Object R 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: RemoteHost, ExternalPort, PortMappingProtocol. Port mapping table for IPv6 Portmappings.
 - 7.10
PortMappingEnabled boolean R 
Portmapping state.
 - 7.10
PortMappingLeaseDuration unsignedInt R 
F!OS IPv6 portmappings are static; parameter always returns "0"
 - 7.10
RemoteHost string R 
Always returns "".
 - 7.10
ExternalPort unsignedInt R
- 7.10
ExternalPortEndRange unsignedInt R
- 7.10
InternalPort unsignedInt R
- 7.10
PortMappingProtocol string R 
Enumeration of:
- TCP
- UDP
 - 7.10
InternalClient string(256) R 
Since F!OS does not use NAT for IPv6, the internal client is always a globally unique IPv6 address.
 - 7.10
PortMappingDescription string(256) R
- 7.10
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.DS_Lite. Object R 
Dual Stack Lite. To activate DS-Lite the IPv6 Mode must be set to one of the native modes: set "InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.Mode" to "ipv6_native" or  "ipv6_native_dhcp6" or "ipv6_native_prefix" or "ipv6_native_static".
 - 6.20
Mode string RW 
Enumeration of:
- dslite_off
- dslite_AFTRbyDHCP6 (get AFTR by DHCPv6)
- dslite_AFTRstatic (set AFTR manually)
 - 6.20
AFTR string(64) RW 
Returns the currently used AFTR. 
To use a AFTR other than assigned by DHCPv6 set Mode="dslite_AFTRstatic" and a static AFTR IPv6 address
 - 6.20
Use_GW_as_PCPServer boolean RW 
Use Gateway as PCP Server
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.IPv6_6rd. Object R 
6rd Tunnel. To activate 6rd tunnel the IPv6 Mode must be set to "ipv6_tunnel_6rd":  "InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.Mode" = "ipv6_tunnel_6rd"
 - 6.20
IPv4Endpoint string RW 
IPv4 address of tunnel endpoint.
 - 6.20
IPv4Masqlen unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
IPv6Prefix string RW 
: (Notation: 2001:12:34:56::/64)
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.IPv6_6in4. Object R 
6in4 Tunnel. To activate 6in4 tunnel the IPv6 Mode must be set to "ipv6_tunnel_6in4":  "InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.Mode" = "ipv6_tunnel_6in4"
 - 6.20
IPv4Endpoint string RW 
IPv4 address of tunnel endpoint.
 - 6.20
IPv6Endpoint string RW 
IPv6 address of tunnel endpoint.
 - 6.20
IPv6LocalAddr string RW 
IPv6 local address..
Changed to InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.1.IPv6_6in4.IPv6LocalAddr
from 
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.1.IPv6_6in4.IPv6LocalAddress
 - 6.20
IPv6Prefix string RW 
: (Notation: 2001:12:34:56::/64)
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.IPv6static. Object R 
Static userdefined IPv6 configuration.. To activate static configuration the IPv6 Mode must be set to "ipv6_native_static":  "InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.Mode" = "ipv6_native_static".
 - 6.20
LANPrefix string RW 
: (Notation: 2001:12:34:56::/64)
 - 6.20
WAN_use_Prefix_of_LAN boolean RW 
Use first 64 prefix of LAN.
 - 6.20
WANPrefix string RW 
: (Notation: 2001:12:34:56::/64)
 - 6.20
WANIfaceID_auto boolean RW 
Assign WAN-InterfaceID automatically.
 - 6.20
WANIfaceID string RW 
Manually set WAN-InterfaceId. (notation: ::1234:2345:6677:7347)
 - 6.20
DNS1 string RW 
IPv6 address of first DNS Server
 - 6.20
DNS2 string RW 
IPv6 address of second DNS Server
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.LAN. Object R 
LAN IPv6 Configuation (Homenet).
 - 6.20
Allow_other_Prefixes boolean RW 
Allow advertised prefixes of other IPv6 routers in LAN.
 - 6.20
RA_adv_DNS boolean RW 
Advertise DNSv6 Server via Router Advertisement in LAN. (RFC 5006).
 - 6.20
ULAMode string RW 
Specifies the assignment of ULA to devices. Enumeration of
"ulamode_off": do not assign ULA
"ulamode_dynamic": assign ULA as long as no IPv6 connection has been established
"ulamode_persist": always assign ULA
 - 6.20
ULAPrefix string R 
Currently used ULA-Prefix of F!Box.
 - 6.20
ULAPrefixOverride string RW 
Use static ULA Prefix for F!Box : (Notation: fd01:1212:3434:5656::/64). Reset to automatic configuration by value = "" or "::"
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.LAN.DHCP6Server. Object R 
Configuration of DHCPv6 Server in LAN.
 - 6.20
Mode string RW 
Possible DHCPv6 modes [RA: O-Flag, M-Flag]:
"off" [O-0, M-0, no answer on requests]
"stateless" [O-1, M-0, only info requests]
"stateful" [O-1, M-1, ia_na and ia_pd]
"onlyprefixes" [O-1, M-0, only ia_pd]
"off_stateless" [O-1, M-0, no answer]
"off_stateful" [O-1, M-1, no answer]
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.MAP. Object R 
Mapping of Address and Port using Translation (MAP-T)  and Mapping of Address and Port with Encapsulation (MAP-E)
 - 7.90
Enabled boolean RW 
Enable MAP-T/MAP-E. Note: .X_AVM-DE_IPv6.{i}.Mode must be ipv6_native.
 0 7.90
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_ParallelConfig. Object R 
Allows configuring parallel and fallback operation modes.

For the currently used wan access type, please check: 
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANCommonInterfaceConfig.WANAccessType
 - 7.10
Enable boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
Only on 6890. Enable or disable parallel or fallback operation mode.
 false 7.10
UseMobileAsPrimary boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
Only on 6890. If set to true, the box will use the mobile/LTE connection as primary connection.
 false 7.10
UseSecondaryAsFallback boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
Only on 6890.
