If set to true, the box will use the secondary connection only as fallback, e.g. when the primary connection fails.
If set to false, the box will use both connections in parallel.
 false 7.10
Mode string RW 
Backup/parallel operation mode
Enumeration of:
- off
- backup
- parallel (OPTIONAL)
 - 8.10
AccessMediaList string R 
Comma-separated list. Lists all allowed access media for Secondary
 - 8.10
Primary string R 
Access medium for the primary internet connection.
The value of this parameter is automatically derived from the general internet settings.
Enumeration of:
- dsl
- cable
- fiber
- ipclient
- wlan
- mobile
- usb
- LAN:X (X is the lan-port number, eg. LAN:1 - LAN:4)
- WAN:1
 - 8.10
Secondary string RW 
Access medium for the secondary internet connection.
Must be one of the values in AccessMediaList or an empty string.
 - 8.10
Downtime unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Time between primary connection loss and switch to the backup connection .
Default is 0, which implies an immediate switch.
 0 7.20
Reverttime unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Time between primary connection reconnect and switch from the backup connection to the primary connection.
Default is 0, which implies an immediate switch.
 0 7.20
MultiserviceFallback boolean RW 
If the "2-Box Operation Mode" is active, multiservice operation can be activated or deactivated by setting this variable.
 false 8.10
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_AdvancedSettings. Object R 
AVM vendor specific object
 - 6.20
1TR114Patch boolean RW
- 6.20
Annex string RW 
DSL-Annex. Supported standards: "A" and "B". Reboot required.
 - 6.20
ATAMode_GUI_hidden boolean RW 
Hide connectiontypes in WebGUI.
 - 6.20
AvailableCountries string R 
Comma sep. String of supported countries as specified by ISO 3166. Only available on boxes with MULTI_COUNTRY supprot. (99=other country).
 - 6.20
BNG_Arch boolean RW 
indicates if BNG Architecture exists
 - 6.50
Upstream_optimize boolean RW 
Upstream optimization. Functionality is hardware dependent.
 false 7.10
Upstream_optimize_count unsignedInt RW 
Number of bundled Ack frames. Functionality is hardware dependent.
 4 7.10
Upstream_optimize_period unsignedInt RW 
Time clock for bundled Ack frames. Functionality is hardware dependent.
 1 7.10
Country string RW 
The value MUST be one value selected
from "AvailableCountries".  Only available on boxes with MULTI_COUNTRY support. (99=other country). Reboot required.
 - 6.20
Disable_Buttons boolean RW 
Deactivate all hardware pushbuttons events.
 - 6.20
DSLPerformance unsignedInt R 
DEPRECATED
Please use the following parameter instead:
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.DSLPerformance.OffsetDsMargin
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.DSLPerformance.DsINP
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.DSLPerformance.RFI_mode
InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANDSLInterfaceConfig.DSLPerformance.UsNoiseBits

Indicates the DSL performace/stability. Allowed configuration  values: 0 ... 40
0   -> max. performance
40 -> max. stability
(only available on xDSL-FRITZ!Box)
 - 6.20
DynDNSEnabled boolean R 
Indicates if Dynamic DNS has been enabled
 - 6.20
FeatureSwitch string RW 
Support for features WLAN and DECT. Following bitmask describes settings:
Write access  "0" : all features  on. 
Write access  "0x001" : Feature WLAN off.
Write access  "0x008" : Feature DECT off.
Read access will deliver a empty string. Parameter is OEM and hardware specific.
 - 6.50
GUIExpertmode boolean R 
Indicates if WebGUI expertmode has been configured
 - 6.20
InfoLED string R 
Delivers a user readable string how the info LED has been mapped
 - 6.20
IPv4NeededOnWan string R 
Returns a hexadecimal Bitmask which describes all services requireing IPv4. Each bit representing its service:
HTTPS_REMOTE_ACCESS 0x0001
FTP_NAS_FORWARD 0x0002
EXPOSED_HOST 0x0004
FORWARD_FOR_LAN_ACCESS 0x0008
OWN_FORWORD_FROM_LANCLIENT_POSSIBLE 0x0010
SMART_HOME 0x0020
VPN 0x0040
 - 6.80
IPv6_GUI_hidden boolean RW 
Hide IPv6 WebGUI page
 - 6.20
LANBridges_GUI_hidden boolean RW 
Hide LAN-Bridges WebGUI page
 - 6.20
LineId string RW 
ACS can set an identifier of used line.
Id-String will be displayed in GUI (depends on hardware)
 - 6.50
NightServiceForRingBlock boolean R 
Indicates if ring block nightservice has been configured
 - 6.20
NightServiceForWLAN boolean R 
Indicates if WLAN nightservice has been configured
 - 6.20
Onlinegames_max_dsrate_kbit unsignedInt RW
- 7.00
Onlinegames_optimize boolean RW
- 7.00
EthernetPrioMark_TR069 int[-1:7] RW 
VLAN priority for outgoing TR-069 traffic

-1 means that the VLAN-Prio of the associated WANConnection is used.
 - 7.90
Prioritisation string R 
User readable comma-seperated string that indicates the prioritisation of applications.

The priorities could contain the following values: 
"prio_none", "prio_veryhigh", "prio_high", "prio_medium", "prio_low"

The user is able to define the following priorties on the web-interface:
Real-time applications: prio_veryhigh
Prior applications: prio_high
Background applications: prio_low

Example:
Internettelefonie=prio_veryhigh, FRITZ!Media Videostreaming=prio_veryhigh, HTTP-Server=prio_veryhigh, eMule=prio_high, Telnet=prio_low
 - 6.20
VoWiFiDNSDomains string(512) RW 
Voice over WiFi: comma separated list of DNS Domains of ePDG endpoints. Wildcards can also be used e.g. "epdg.epc.*.3gppnetwork.org, epdg.dnsdomain2.org"
 - 7.50
ProviderDefaultConfigVersion string R 
Version of specific Providerprofile.
 - 6.20
ProviderAdditiveProfile string R 
Returns the name of the Provider Additive Profile. If no Profile is installed an empty string will be returned.
 <empty> 7.55
PVC2_GUI_hidden boolean RW 
Hide ATM config of 2. PVC (voip) in WebGUI. (only relevant for xDSL capable boxes)
 - 6.20
Country_GUI_hidden boolean RW 
Hide Country settings in WebGUI.
 false 7.25
Support1TR114Patch boolean RW 
Reset SIP VLAN Prio to 0 in 'Deutsche Telekom' resale architecture (default is VLAN Prio = 6). This parameter is available only in VDSL capable boxes.
 - 6.20
WANMAC string RW 
Read: MAC used for WAN internet connection
Write: use this MAC.( notation aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff)
 - 6.20
WANMACManagement string R 
only for Docsis Devices: Cable Modem MAC address
 - 7.20
WANMACOverride boolean RW 
Indicates if the WANMAC is the CPE's default MAC. Set to false to use the CPE's default MAC. Value true can't be written. Setting this value may lead to temporary loss of internet connection.
 - 6.20
DVBCEnabled boolean RW 
Toggles DVB-C streaming.
WARNING: Changing the value of this parameter will cause a device reboot.
 - 7.50
Use_Public_DNS_Servers_As_Fallback boolean RW 
The parameter enables or disables the public DNS server fallback. Once it is enabled the availability of all UDP DNSv4 and DNSv6 server is tracked regarding their ability to provide a valid DNS record. In case none of the configured servers is able to send a valid reply to a probing query within 30 seconds, the public fallback is activated. As a result incoming DNS traffic is forwarded to permanently defined public servers with a high availability of the following providers (Google, Cloudflare and Quad9). While being activated, the availability of the configured DNS servers is verified in an interval of four hours. In case a DNS server is again able to send a valid reply the public fallback is deactivated. 
List of public DNSv4 and DNSv6 fallback servers: 
Google: 8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4, 2001:4860:4860::8888, 2001:4860:4860::8844, 
Cloudflare: 1.1.1.1, 1.0.0.1, 2606:4700:4700::1111, 2606:4700:4700::1001, 
Quad9: 9.9.9.9, 149.112.112.112, 2620:fe::fe, 2620:fe::9.
 true 7.50
Use_EDNS0_ClientIdentificationMacAndHostname boolean RW 
Enable Extended DNS with MAC and hostname identification.
 false 7.70
CPU_Kick boolean RW 
Not available on all FRITZ! platforms. Change of CPU power mode. Contact AVM for more information.
 false 7.70
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_AdvancedSettings.DNSoverTLS. Object R
- 7.20
Enabled boolean RW 
Enable the flag to activate DNSoverTLS. Disable it to deactivate the feature
 false 7.20
Strict boolean RW 
There are two different operation modes for the DNSoverTLS feature (strict and
opportunistic). If the flag is enabled, the strict mode is activated otherwise the
opportunistic mode is activated.

strict mode:
If the flag is enabled, only DNSoverTLS server with a valid certificate chain and a
valid FQDN-CN or FQDN-SAN matching are allowed. If one of both validation steps fail
then the DNSoverTLS server is deactivated.

opportunistic mode:
If the flag is disabled, DNSoverTLS servers are used even if the certificate chain
validation step fails. In this case an opportunistic TLS connection to the server is
established without knowing if the server is who it says it is.

Attention: This mode should only be activated if the identity of the server is well
known and a man in the middle attack can be precluded.
 true 7.20
UDP_Fallback boolean RW 
If none of the specified DNSoverTLS servers works as expected, a fallback to UDP can
be performed. This means that only the configured UDP-based DNS servers are used.

If the flag is enabled then the fallback is allowed. Otherwise it is not.

Attention: If the flag is disabled and all DNSoverTLS servers fail, no DNS requests
can be sent anymore.
 true 7.20
FQDNs string(1:4096) RW 
Comma-separated list. Valid FQDNs. The list is used to resolve the IP adresses
of the DNSoverTLS servers. Depending on whether IPv6 has been enabled,IPv4 only
or IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are resolved. The resolve is executed by the DNS servers
provided via DSL/Cable.

For example: "dns.google,dns.quad9.net" for the DoT servers of Google and Quad9.

After the certificate chain has been validated during the common TLS validation
process, the FQDNs are checked against the Common Name (CN) and Subject Alternative
Names (SANs).
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_Budget. Object R 
ACS controlled bandwidth regulation
 - 6.20
UserBudget unsignedInt RW 
Allowed User Budget in MB, only informative
 - 6.20
VolumeCount unsignedInt R 
Current Volume Count in MB since last reset
 - 6.20
ResetVolumeCount boolean RW 
Reset VolumeCount, allowed value=1
 - 6.20
BandwithRegulation boolean RW 
1=active, 0=not active. NOTE: activating BandwithRegulation is not retained across reboots.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_SpeedtestServer. Object R 
Starts an iperf server on the CPE for TCP and UDP speedtests. For detailed information about Speedtest Measuring see separate document.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_SpeedtestServer.TCP. Object R 
TCP iperf server
 - 6.20
Start unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this Parameter will start TCP Server
 - 6.20
Stop unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this Parameter will stop TCP Server
 - 6.20
Reset unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this Parameter will reset statistic.
 - 6.50
Port unsignedInt RW 
TCP Listen port for speedtest server
 4711 6.20
Result string R 
User readable string with the byte and packet statistic of the current or last speedtest.
Example "Bytes 4803924 \t BPS 104501 \t AVG 116106 Packets 3294 PPS 26 AVG 9"
 - 6.20
State string R 
User readable string returning the state of the speedtest server.
For ex.:  "TCP 4711 connected UDP off".
 - 6.20
WANAccess boolean RW 
Open TCP port for WAN Access
 false 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_SpeedtestServer.UDP. Object R 
UDP iperf server
 - 6.20
DurationDownstreamTest unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Duration of downstream test.
For upstream only tests set this parameter to 0.
 - 6.50
DurationUpstreamTest unsignedInt RW 
DEPRECATED
Unit: seconds. Duration of upstream test.
For downstream only tests set this paramter to 0.

Note: The parameter is deprecated. For a clean upstream test use the 'IPLayerCapacity' diagnostics test (TR471, Role: 'Sender').
With the classic 'InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_SpeedtestServer' you could try to use the 'UDP.StartBidirect' test method.
 - 6.50
Port unsignedInt RW 
UDP Listen port for speedtest server
 4711 6.20
PortBidirect unsignedInt RW 
UDP Listen port for bidirectional speedtest server.
Received data on this port will be sent back to sender.
 4712 6.20
Reset unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this parameter will reset statistic.
 - 6.50
Result string R 
User readable string with the byte and packet statistic of the current or last speedtest.

After command "Start":                  "Bytes 4803924 \t BPS 104501 \t AVG 116106 Packets 3294 PPS 26 AVG 9"
After command "StartSpeedtest":  "Downstream (kbit/s)\tAVG 45333\tMIN 2367\tMAX 64578"
 - 6.20
Start unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this parameter will start UDP server
 - 6.20
StartBidirect unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this parameter will start fullduplex UDP server. Box will transmit received data back to sender.
 - 6.20
StartSpeedtest unsignedInt RW 
Start Speedtest.
 - 6.50
State string R 
User readable string returning the state of the speedtest server. For ex.:  "TCP 4711 connected UDP off"
 - 6.20
Stop unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this parameter will stop UDP server
 - 6.20
StopBidirect unsignedInt RW 
Write access to this parameter will stop UDP bidirect server.
 - 6.20
StopSpeedtest unsignedInt RW 
Stop Speedtest.
 - 6.50
WANAccess boolean RW 
Open UDP port for WAN Access
 false 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_IGMP. Object R 
IGMP Settings in FRITZ!OS
 - 7.50
IGMPVersionForUpstream int RW 
IGMP Version
 3 7.50
IGMPVersionForHomenet int RW 
Used IGMP Version in Homenet
 3 7.50
IGMPDSCP int RW 
DSCP Mark for IGMP
 48 7.50
IGMPSSM boolean RW 
Enable Source-specific multicast (SSM)
 1 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools. Object R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.TraceRouteDiagnostics. Object R 
invoke a traceroute test on the CPE
 - 6.20
DiagnosticsState string RW 
Indicates availability of traceroute diagnostic data. One of:
"None"
"Requested"
"Timeout"
"Complete"
Value may be set to Requested to initiate the diagnostic test. When writing, the only allowed value is Requested. To ensure the use of the proper test parameters (the writable parameters in this object), the test parameters MUST be set either prior to or at the same time as (in the same SetParameterValues) setting the DiagnosticState to Requested.
When requested, CPE will wait until after completion of the communication session with the ACS before starting the diagnostic. When the diagnostic initiated by the ACS is completed (successfully or not), the CPE will establish a new connection to the ACS to allow the ACS to view the "Result", indicating the Event code "8 DIAGNOSTICS COMPLETE" in the Inform message.
 - 6.20
ProtocolVersion string RW 
Describes rather IPv4 or IPv6 is used
Enumeration of:
- IPv4
- IPv6
 IPv4 6.20
Host string RW 
Hostname or IPv4 address
 - 6.20
NumberOfTries unsignedInt RW 
Number of probes per TTL (default 3)
 - 6.20
Timeout unsignedInt RW 
Timeout in seconds for the traceroute test (default 20).
 - 6.20
MaxHopCount unsignedInt RW 
Max number of hops (default 64)
 - 6.20
Result string R 
The result of the traceroute test. S.th. like 
"traceroute to avm.de (212.42.244.80), 30 hops max, 38 byte packets
1  ewnw-pppoe.blabla.de (88.234.237.139)  15.355 ms  15.704 ms  15.321 ms
2  ewnw-gate.blabla.de (88.234.237.144)  15.837 ms  15.767 ms  15.580 ms
3  88.234.237.2 (88.234.237.2)  16.502 ms  16.427 ms  16.750 ms
..."
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.NSLookupDiagnostics. Object R 
invoke a nslookup test on the CPE
 - 6.20
DiagnosticsState string RW 
Indicates availability of nslookup diagnostic data. One of:
"None"
"Requested"
"Timeout"
"Complete"
Value may be set to Requested to initiate the diagnostic test. When writing, the only allowed value is Requested. To ensure the use of the proper test parameters (the writable parameters in this object), the test parameters MUST be set either prior to or at the same time as (in the same SetParameterValues) setting the DiagnosticState to Requested.
When requested, CPE will wait until after completion of the communication session with the ACS before starting the diagnostic. When the diagnostic initiated by the ACS is completed (successfully or not), the CPE will establish a new connection to the ACS to allow the ACS to view the "Result", indicating the Event code "8 DIAGNOSTICS COMPLETE" in the Inform message.
 - 6.20
Interface string RW 
Specifies the interface to use for the DNS lookup. For ex. "InternetGatewayDevice.WANConnection.1.WANConnectionDevice.1.WANPPPConnection.1.". If an empty string is specified the default internet connection is used.
 - 6.20
Host string RW 
Hostname
 - 6.20
DNSServer string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address of the DNS server to use. If an empty string is specified, the device's default DNS server will be used.
 - 6.20
Timeout unsignedInt RW 
Timeout in seconds for the nslookup test (default 10).
 - 6.20
Result string R 
The result of the nslookup test. S.th. like:

Server: 2a01:198:24f:3004::2
Name: www.ix.de
Address 1: 193.99.144.80
Address 2: 2a02:2e0:3fe:100::8

responsetime: 19 ms
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.IPLayerCapacity. Object R 
This object defines the IP Layer Capacity measurement configuration.
IP Layer Capacity measurement is specified in [TR-471].

Data received in the IP Layer Capacity measurement do not require storage on the device.

FRITZ!OS implements the OB-UDPST utility to do UDP-based IP capacity measurements. 
All specified parameter of  this object will be used together with this implementation.
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.IPLayerCapacity.Config. Object R
- 7.50
Host string(255) RW 
The Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) or IP address of the Test Endpoint to perform the UDP Capacity tests with.

udpst-parameter: server
 - 7.50
Port unsignedInt[1:65535] RW 
Port on the Test Endpoint host.

udpst-option: -p
 25000 7.50
Role string RW 
Indicates whether the device will act as Sender or Receiver of test packets.
For downstream tests select 'Receiver' for upstream tests 'Sender' (FBox point-of-view)

udpst-option: -u|-d
Enumeration of:
- Receiver (The device will act as the Receiver.)
- Sender (The device will act as the Sender.)
 Receiver 7.50
JumboFramesPermitted boolean RW 
If true, jumbo frames are allowed above 1 Gbps.

udpst-option: -j   (attention: opposite meaning)
 true 7.50
EthernetPriority unsignedInt[0:255] RW 
Packet marking octet (IP_TOS/IPV6_TCLASS)

udpst-option: -m
 0 7.50
ProtocolVersion string RW 
Indicates the IP protocol version to be used.

udpst-options: -4|-6
Enumeration of:
- Any (Use either IPv4 or IPv6 depending on the system preference.)
- IPv4 (Use IPv4 for the requests)
- IPv6 (Use IPv6 for the requests.)
 Any 7.50
UDPPayloadContent string RW 
UDP Payload Content Type, If there is payload compression in the
path and tests intend to characterize a possible advantage due to
compression, then payload content SHOULD be supplied by a
pseudo-random sequence generator, by using part of a compressed
file, or by other means. Payload may also contain the test
protocol PDUs.

udpst-option: -X   (for random content)
Enumeration of:
- zeroes (All zero (0) payload content.)
- random (Random payload content.)
 zeroes 7.50
TestType string RW 
Indicates the type of IP-Layer Capacity test being run.

Specifies how the parameter 'StartSendingRateIndex' is applied.
For TestType 'Fixed', the data rate index specified is used as the static data rate throughout the test.
For the TestType 'Search' the data rate index specified is used as the start data rate, which is automatically increased slowly.

udpst-option: -I [@]index       (together with 'StartSendingRateIndex')
Enumeration of:
- Search (Search algorithm will be used to determine sending rate.)
- Fixed (Fixed sending rate will be used.)
 Search 7.50
IPDVEnable boolean RW 
Enables one-way IPDV (IP Packet Delay Variation) metric for load
rate adjustment algorithm. When true (enabled), one-way delay
variation is used, otherwise round-trip delay variation is used.

udpst-option: -o
 false 7.50
NumberFirstModeTestSubIntervals unsignedInt[0:3600] RW 
When the value >= 1, bimodal test mode is requested and the
value represents the number of sub-intervals to be included in
the first capacity test mode. 

Value 0 = indicates that bimodal testing is disabled (default).

udpst-option: -i
 0 7.50
StatusFeedbackInterval unsignedInt[5:250] RW 
Unit: milliseconds. Period of status feedback message (receiver of offered load
returns messages to the sender with results of measured metrics).
Value specified in milliseconds.

udpst-option: -F
 50 7.50
SeqErrThresh unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. Threshold for Loss or Reordering or
Replication impairments measured (events where received packet
sequence number did not increase by one).

udpst-option: -q
 10 7.50
ReordDupIgnoreEnable boolean RW 
This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. When true (enabled) only Loss
counts toward received packet sequence number errors, and
reordering and Duplication impairments are ignored. When
false (disabled), Loss, Reordering and Duplication are all
counted as sequence number errors.

udpst-option: -R
 false 7.50
LowerThresh unsignedInt[1:10000] RW 
Unit: milliseconds. Low delay variation threshold.

This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. The lower threshold on the range of
Round Trip Time (RTT) variation.

udpst-option: -L
 30 7.50
UpperThresh unsignedInt[1:10000] RW 
Unit: milliseconds. Upper delay variation threshold.

This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. The upper threshold on the range of
Round Trip Time (RTT) variation.

udpst-option: -U
 90 7.50
HighSpeedDelta unsignedInt[1:255] RW 
Unit: rows. High-speed (row adjustment) delta.

This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. The number of rows to move in a single
adjustment when initially increasing offered load (to ramp up
quickly).

udpst-option: -h
 10 7.50
SlowAdjThresh unsignedInt[1:65535] RW 
Congestion slow adjustment threshold.

This parameter is only meaningful if {{param|TestType}} is
{{enum|Search|TestType}}. Threshold on the measured number of
consecutive status reports indicating loss and/or delay variation
above {{param|UpperThresh}}.

udpst-option: -c
 3 7.50
TestIntervalSecs unsignedInt[5:30] RW 
Unit: seconds. Test interval time in seconds

udpst-option: -t
 10 7.50
TestSubIntervalSecs unsignedInt[1:10] RW 
Unit: seconds. Duration of intermediate measurement reporting intervals (Sub-interval period)

udpst-option: -P
 1 7.50
StartSendingRateIndex unsignedInt[0:1090] RW 
Substitutes the parameter "StartSendingRate".

The Sending Rate for a {{enum|Fixed|TestType}} test or the initial Sending Rate value for a {{enum|Search|TestType}} test.

vendor-specific:
Instead of the concrete Sending Rate the controller has to pass a Sending Rate Index.
A table of all available Sending Rates together with their index values is available by calling "udpst -S"  (0..1090 possibilities)

udpst-option: -I [@]index       (together with 'TestType')
 0 7.50
SocketBufferSize unsignedInt[0:16777216] RW 
Socket buffer request size (SO_SNDBUF/SO_RCVBUF)
(0 = System default/minimum)

udpst-option: -b
 1024000 7.50
DatagramSizeTraditional boolean RW 
Use datagram sizes for traditional (1500 byte) MTU

udpst-option: -T
 false 7.50
MaxBandwidth unsignedInt[0:10000] RW 
Unit: mbps. Max bandwidth supported by the client OR available to server
0 (default) means this value is not known and cannot be passed 

udpst-option: -B   (if != 0)
 0 7.50
AuthKey string(32) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value. Authentication key to authenticate at udpst-server daemon
If empty, no authentication is used.

Allowed chars:
a to Z, the digits from 0 to 9, the special characters: '.', ':', '(', ')'

udpst-option: -a
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.IPLayerCapacity.Result. Object R
- 7.50
Success boolean R 
Indicates whether the udpst command was executed successfully
If "true" the 'Result" parameter contains the json-result of udpst.
If "false" the "Message" parameter should contain an plain error message.

Note:
The json-result can also refer to errors during the test run, eg.: a failed DNS resolution.
In this case "Success" is also set to "true", but the "Result" parameter will contain an error-json object.
 - 7.50
Result string R 
Contains the json-result of udpst.

Example (successful test run):

{
   "IPLayerMaxConnections":1,
   "IPLayerMaxIncrementalResult":3600,
   "IPLayerCapSupported":{
      "SoftwareVersion":"7.4.0",
      "ControlProtocolVersion":9,
      "Metrics":"IPLR,Sampled_RTT,IPDV,IPRR,RIPR"
   },
   "Input":{  ... /* all input params */ .... },
   "Output":{
      "BOMTime":"2022-11-18T09:15:43.296359Z",
      "TmaxUsed":1000,
      "TestInterval":5,
      "TmaxRTTUsed":3000,
      "TimestampResolutionUsed":1,
      "IncrementalResult":[ { /* ...results of every test interval... */ }, ... ],
      "Summary":                { /* ...results summary params... */ },
      "AtMax":                    { /* ...maxima measrued during the test... */ },
      "ModalResult":[ ],
      "EOMTime":"2022-11-18T09:15:48.860740Z",
      "Status":"Complete"
   },
   "ErrorStatus":0,
   "ErrorMessage":""
}

Example (failed test run, here: DNS failure):
{
   "IPLayerMaxConnections":1,
   "IPLayerMaxIncrementalResult":3600,
   "IPLayerCapSupported":{
      "SoftwareVersion":"7.4.0",
      "ControlProtocolVersion":9,
      "Metrics":"IPLR,Sampled_RTT,IPDV,IPRR,RIPR"
   },
   "ErrorStatus":-1,
   "ErrorMessage":"GETADDRINFO ERROR: Interrupted system call (Name does not resolve)"
}
 - 7.50
Message string R 
May contain an additional error/warning message if an unexpected problem has occurred.
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.IPLayerCapacity.Control. Object R
- 7.50
Start boolean RW 
Starts a IPLayerCapacity-Test with the configuration passed in object 'Config'.
Can be used in State "Ready" or "Finished".

Note:
Make sure to configure at least the host paramater (.. .IPLayerCapacity.Config.Host) before starting a test run.
All other configuration parameters are preconfigured with default values.
 - 7.50
Abort boolean RW 
Aborts a running IPLayerCapacityTest.
Can be used in State "Running".
 - 7.50
State string R 
Returns the current state of an IPLayerCapacity test
Enumeration of:
- Uninitialized (Please configure the IPLayerCapacity-Test with the params in object 'Config' first)
- Ready (The IPLayerCapacity-Test is configured and ready to be started by command 'Start'.

Note: The "Ready" state is then no longer entered after the first test run has been completed.)
- Running (An instance of the IPLayerCapacity-Test is currently running)
- Finished (A IPLayerCapacity-Test has be executed, you can retrieve the results from object 'Result'.

You can also start a new test run within this status (like in "Ready").)
 - 7.50
IPLayerCapSupportedSoftwareVersion string(64) R 
Indicates the installed version of the test software. The software
version string will be implementation-dependent, and SHOULD identify
both the implementation and the version (e.g., "7.4.0").
 - 7.50
IPLayerCapSupportedControlProtocolVersion string(64) R 
Indicates the control protocol version supported by the test
software. Refer to [TR-471] for more information (e.g. "8")
 - 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_DiagnosticTools.IPTV. Object R 
IPTV and Multicast Diagnostics.
 - 6.50
ConnectionName string R 
Name of internet connection over which IPTV runs. ConnectionName corresponds with "InternetGatewayDevice.WANDevice.{i}.WANConnectionDevice.{i}.WANIPConnection.{i}.Name".
 - 6.50
LastMCfromWAN dateTime R 
Timestamp of last seen Multicast from WAN.
 - 6.50
MCServer string R 
IP-Address of Multicast Server.
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi. Object R 
WiFi: Accesspoint and Hotspot configuration
 - 6.20
RadioNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
The number of entries in the Radio table.
 - 6.20
SSIDNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
The number of entries in the SSID table.
 - 6.20
AccessPointNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
The number of entries in the AccessPoint table.
 - 6.20
GRENumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
The number of entries in the GRE table.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.ACLConfig. Object R
- 6.20
AutoFillEnabled boolean RW 
true: As soon as a Wi-Fi client has successfully connected to an SSID from the list "Private Network" the MAC address of the respective Wi-Fi client is added automatically to each ACL of those SSIDs which are in the list "Public Network". 
This procedure is referred to as "Automated Filling of WLAN ACLs". Currently we distinguish only between all AP's in a private network and all AP's in a public network.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.Radio.{i}. Object R 
Number of preinstanciated objects depends on hardware.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enables or disables the radio. Default is true.
Implementation status: write access is ignored
 - 6.20
Status string R 
The current operational state of the radio. Enumeration of ["Up" "Down" "Unknown" "Dormant" "NotPresent" "LowerLayerDown" "Error"]:
 - 6.20
Name string R 
The textual name of the radio as assigned by FRITZ!Box.
 - 6.20
Upstream boolean R 
Indicates whether the interface points towards the Internet (true) or towards End Devices (false).
 - 6.20
MaxBitRate unsignedInt R 
The maximum PHY bit rate supported by this interface (expressed in Mbps).
 - 6.20
SupportedFrequencyBands string R 
Comma-separated list. List items indicate the frequency bands at which the radio can operate.
Enumeration of:
- 2.4GHz
- 5GHz
 - 6.20
OperatingFrequencyBand string RW 
The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the SupportedFrequencyBands parameter. Indicates the frequency band at which the radio is operating.
Implementation status: OperationFrequencyBand depends on configured channel. Write access is ignored.
 - 6.20
PossibleChannels string(1024) R 
Comma-separated list. List items represent possible radio channels for the wireless standard (a, b, g, n) and the regulatory domain.
 - 6.20
ChannelsInUse string(1024) R 
Comma-separated list. List items represent channels that the radio determines to be currently in use (including any that it is using itself).
 - 6.20
Channel unsignedInt RW 
The current radio channel used by the connection. To request automatic channel selection, set AutoChannelEnable to true.
 - 6.20
AutoChannelSupported boolean R 
Indicates whether automatic channel selection is supported by this radio. If false, then AutoChannelEnable MUST be false.
 - 6.20
AutoChannelEnable boolean RW 
Enable or disable automatic channel selection.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.Radio.{i}.Stats. Object R 
Radio statistics. Not supported yet, all parameters return '0'.
 - 6.20
BytesReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
BytesSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
DiscardPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
DiscardPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
ErrorsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
ErrorsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
PacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.20
PacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.SSID.{i}. Object RW 
For each Accesspoint a SSID object must be added by method 'AddObject'. By default there is no SSID object existent.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enables or disables the SSID entry.
Implementation status: write access is ignored. Disabling this entry can only be deployed by disabling the apropriate Accesspoint-Object.
 - 6.20
Status string R 
The current operational state of the SSID entry. Enumeration of: [ "Up" "Down" "Unknown" "Dormant" "NotPresent" "LowerLayerDown" "Error"]
 - 6.20
Name string R 
The textual name of the SSID entry as assigned by FRITZ!Box.
 - 6.20
BSSID string R 
[MACAddress] The Basic Service Set ID.
 - 6.20
MACAddress string R 
[MACAddress] The MAC address of this interface.
 - 6.20
SSID string RW 
The current service set identifier in use by the connection. The SSID is an identifier that is attached to packets sent over the wireless LAN that functions as an ID for joining a particular radio network (BSS).
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.SSID.{i}.Stats. Object R 
SSID statistics.
 - 6.50
BytesSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
BytesReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
PacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
PacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
ErrorsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
ErrorsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
DiscardPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
DiscardPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
UnicastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
MulticastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
MulticastPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
BroadcastPacketsSent unsignedInt R
- 6.50
BroadcastPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
UnknownProtoPacketsReceived unsignedInt R
- 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.AccessPoint.{i}. Object RW 
For each WiFi-Accesspoint  a "AccessPoint"-object must be added by method 'AddObject'. By default there is no AccessPoint object existent. Each AccessPoint object must have a valid 'RadioReference' and 'SSIDReference'.
 - 6.20
OperationMode string RW 
The only supported OperationMode is currently "hotspot".
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enables or disables this access point.
 - 6.20
Status string R 
Indicates the status of this access point. Enumeration of ["Disabled" "Enabled" "Error_Misconfigured" "Error"]
 - 6.20
InterfaceName string R 
The device Interface name used for this connection. For configuring a GRE tunnel this name is the reference to the AP.
 - 6.20
SSIDReference int RW 
The value MUST be the instance number of a row in the SSID table.
 - 6.20
RadioReference int RW 
The value MUST be the instance number of a row in the Radio table.
 - 6.20
SSIDAdvertisementEnabled boolean RW 
Indicates whether or not beacons include the SSID name.
 - 6.20
RetryLimit unsignedInt RW 
The maximum number of retransmission for a packet.
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
 - 6.20
MaxAssociatedDevices unsignedInt RW 
The maximum number of devices that can simultaneously be connected to the access point. A value of 0 means that there is no specific limit.
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
 - 6.20
UAPSDCapability boolean R 
Indicates whether this access point supports WMM Unscheduled Automatic Power Save Delivery (U-APSD). Will deliver false.
 - 6.20
UAPSDEnable boolean RW 
Whether U-APSD support is currently enabled. When enabled, this is indicated in beacon frames. Value true will result in error.
 - 6.20
WMMCapability boolean R 
Indicates whether this access point supports WiFi Multimedia (WMM) Access Categories (AC).
 - 6.20
WMMEnable boolean RW 
Whether WMM support is currently enabled. When enabled, this is indicated in beacon frames.
Implementation status: WMM is supported by  default. Write access is ignored.
 - 6.20
IsolationEnable boolean RW 
Enables or disables device isolation.
A value of true means that the devices connected to the Access Point are isolated from all other devices within the home network (as is typically the case for a Wireless Hotspot).
Implementation status: depends on OperationMode. In OperationMode "hotspot" IsolationEnable is always true.
 - 6.20
Powerlevel unsignedInt RW 
1 (100%), 2 (50%), 3 (25%), 4 (12%), 5 (6%)
 - 6.20
Coexistence boolean RW 
Coexistence according IEEE 802.11, 2.4 GHz only
 - 6.20
WirelessMode unsignedInt RW 
Indicates the current mode. Currently only modes 2, 3, 4, 7, 9 are supported.
0: 802.11b
1: 802.11g
2: 802.11bg
3: 802.11bgn
4: 802.11gn
5: 802.11n
6: 802.11a
7: 802.11na
8: 802.11ac
9: 802.11nac
 - 6.20
MACAccessListType unsignedInt RW 
Indicates the type of MACAccessList.
0: blacklist
1: whitelist
 - 6.20
MACAccessList string RW 
Commaseparated list of client MACAddresses which are black- or whitelisted for this accesspoint.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.AccessPoint.{i}.ClientAccessControl. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable Hotspot metric.
 - 6.20
MaxClients unsignedInt RW 
Hotspot metric.
 - 6.20
MinClientRSSI int[0:100] RW 
The minimum RSSI to allow a connection given in per cent.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.AccessPoint.{i}.Hotspot20. Object R 
Wi-Fi Alliance - Hotspot 2.0 (Release 1) Technical Specification Version 1.0.0
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
OperatorFriendlyName string RW 
Hex oder Ascii, Codeing is described by prefix "hex:" or "asc:" Example:  "asc:AVM GmbH"
 - 6.20
HomeRealm string RW 
Identifier of own network. Example: "avm.de"
 - 6.20
HESSID string RW 
Homogeneous ESS Identifier. Eligible MAC ddress for identification of AP group. Example: "00:11:22:33:44:55"
 - 6.20
RoamingConsortium string RW 
OUIs of provider, Hex. Example AVM: 00040E
 - 6.20
VenueName string RW 
UTF-8 coded double of language und name. Example: "eng:AVM GmbH, 10559 Berlin, Germany"
 - 6.20
VenueType unsignedInt RW 
ID 0..255. Venue Type depends on venue group.  Example of group 01: 9=museum, 12=Bar, 13=Cafe
 - 6.20
VenueGroup unsignedInt RW 
ID 0..255. Example 01=public, 03=education, 10=vehicle
 - 6.20
AccessNetworkType unsignedInt RW 
ID  0..15. Access description. 00=Private network, 03=Free public access, 05=Emergency Services
 - 6.20
InternetAccess boolean RW 
True: internet services accessible; false: internet services are not accessible
 - 6.20
ASRA boolean RW 
ASRA: additional steps required for access.
 - 6.20
ESR boolean RW 
ESR: emergency service reachable
 - 6.20
UESA boolean RW 
UESA: unauthenticated emergency service accessible
 - 6.20
RoamingConsortiumList string RW 
OUIs of providers in cooperation.
 - 6.20
NAIRealmList string RW 
- Access Realms to this hotspot. E.g. own realm and those of cooperation networks.
Description:
-- encoding=00 (UTF-8 - fixed)
-- 0..n Realm: realm=example.org
-- 0..n EAP method under IANA
-- AuthID und AuthVal under IEEE802.11u
- Example: "encoding=00realm=example.com;eap_method=12auth_id=02auth_val=04"
 - 6.20
CellularNetworkList string RW 
Identifier of networks in cooperation by PLMNs.
- PLMN = Public Land Mobile Network -> Mobile County Code + Mobile Network Code (MCC+MCN)
- Example: Vodafone Germany 262002 - 262=De, 002=VF
 - 6.20
DomainNameList string RW 
Provider domains. Example: "avm.de;wifi.org"
 - 6.20
NetworkAuthenticationType string RW 
ID describing type of enrollment,where appropriate with URL.
Allowed values:
"00<URL>" = acceptance of terms and conditions (+URL)
"01"           = online enrollement supported
"02<URL>" = http/https redirect (+URL)
"03"           = DNS redirect
- Example: "00https://captivportal.avm.de"
 - 6.50
IPAddrTypeAvailablity unsignedInt RW 
- 1 Byte, Bit 0/1=IPv6, Bit 2-7=IPv4
--IPv6: 0 = Address type not available, IPv6: 1 = Address type available, IPv6: 2 = Availability of the address type not known, IPv6: 3 = Reserved
-- IPv4: 0 = Address type not available, IPv4: 1 = Public IPv4 address available, IPv4: 2 = Port-restricted IPv4 address available, IPv4: 3 = Single NATed private IPv4 address available
-- IPv4: 4 = Double NATed private IPv4 address available,  IPv4: 5 = Port-restricted IPv4 address and single NATed IPv4 address available,  
-- IPv4: 6 = Port-restricted IPv4 address and double NATed IPv4 address available, 
-- IPv4: 7 = Availability of the address type is not known,
-- IPv4: 8 .. 63 = Reserved
- Example "0c"
 - 6.20
ConnectionCapability string RW 
- Hex coded touple of IP-protocol, portnumber and port status, see WFS HS20 Spezification Sec.4.5
 - 1 Octet IP-Prot., 2 Octets Portnumber, 1 Octet Port-Status
 - Example: ICMP closee = 01000000
 - Example: "010000000614000106160000"
 - 6.20
WANMetrics string RW 
Description of the Internet-Uplink, Hex [wan_info, dl_speed, ul_speed, dl_load, ul_load, ldm]
 -- WAN Info: Link Status, Symmetric Link, At Capacity
 -- Downlink Speed, Uplink Speed [kpbs]
 -- Downlink Load, Uplink Load [0..255] scaled in % from 0..100
 -- LDM - Load Measurement Duration [1/10 s]
 - Example: wan_info:01dl_speed:c4090000ul_speed:80010000dl_load:00ul_load:00lmd:0000
 - 6.20
DGAF boolean RW 
- 0/1: enable/disable downlink group address frames. (Similar to WLAN Client Isolation)
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.AccessPoint.{i}.Security. Object R
- 6.20
Reset boolean RW
- 6.20
ModesSupported string R 
Comma-separated list. Indicates which security modes this AccessPoint instance is capable of supporting.
Currently the following modes are supported: [ "None",  "WPA2-Enterprise" ]
 - 6.20
ModeEnabled string RW 
The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the ModesSupported parameter. Indicates which security mode is enabled.
 - 6.20
WEPKey string RW 
DEPRECATED
[obsolete]
For security resasons WEP is not supported anymore.
Not supported in OperationMode "hotspot";String.
Not supported in OperationMode "hotspot";String.
 - 6.20
RekeyingInterval unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerIPAddr1 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address. At least one (1) RadiusServer has to be defined. Additional RadiusServers are optional.
 - 6.20
RadiusServerPort1 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerSecret1 string RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerIPAddr2 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
RadiusServerPort2 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerSecret2 string RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerIPAddr3 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
RadiusServerPort3 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerSecret3 string RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerIPAddr4 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
RadiusServerPort4 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerSecret4 string RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerIPAddr5 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
RadiusServerPort5 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
RadiusServerSecret5 string RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerIPAddr1 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address. Accounting Servers are optional.
 - 6.20
AccountingServerPort1 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerSecret1 string RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerIPAddr2 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
AccountingServerPort2 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerSecret2 string RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerIPAddr3 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
AccountingServerPort3 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerSecret3 string RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerIPAddr4 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
AccountingServerPort4 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerSecret4 string RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerIPAddr5 string RW 
IPv4 or IPv6 address
 - 6.20
AccountingServerPort5 unsignedInt RW
- 6.20
AccountingServerSecret5 string RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.AccessPoint.{i}.Security.WPS. Object R 
Wi-Fi Alliance - Wi-Fi Simple Configuration Technical Specification Version 2.0.1.
WPS is not supported for "...AccessPoint.{i}.OperationMode" = "hotspot".
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
ConfigMethodsSupported string R 
Supported methods are ["Keypad", "PushButton", "Display"].
 - 6.20
ConfigMethodsEnabled string RW 
The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the ConfigMethodsSupported parameter.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.GRE.{i}. Object RW 
Object for setting up one or more GRE tunnel. The Reference to the corresponding AP is the AP's InterfaceName. By default there is no GRE object existent. GRE objects must be added by method AddObject.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable GRE tunnel.
 - 6.20
APReference int RW 
The instance reference to the used Accesspoint (AP).
 - 6.50
InterfaceName string RW 
The interface name of the used Accesspoint (AP). Will be set automatically if APReference is set.
 - 6.20
CircuitId string RW 
CircuitId used for DHCP option 82. Keywords:
$MAC - for including MAC-Address (ascii)
$SSID - SSID 
$SSIDTYPE - currently "0" supported
$0 - binary-0
 - 6.20
VLANEnable boolean RW 
If enabled packets to GRE-Server are VLAN-tagged.
 - 6.20
VLANId int RW 
VLANId.
 - 6.20
VLANPrio int RW 
VLANPrio.
 - 6.20
GREServer string RW 
Tunnel Endpoint GRE Server. IP-Address or a FQDN.
 - 6.20
ProtocolPrefer int[0:4] RW 
IP Protocol to be used or prefered. Allowed values:
0 = IPv4 only
1 = IPv6 only
2 = prefer IPv4
3 = prefer IPv6
4 = both
Default if not OEM specific defined: prefer IPv6  (3)
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.GRE.{i}.DPD. Object R 
DPD (Dead Peer Detection) of GRE server connection.
 - 6.50
Inactivity unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Time period used to check whether an established connection to the GRE server is still alive.
Default value is 20 seconds.
 - 6.50
ReplyWait unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Timeout of ping request(s) used to check the availability of the GRE server.
Default value is 1 second.
 - 6.50
NPings unsignedInt RW 
Number of pings used to check whether the GRE server is available or not.
Default value is 4.
 - 6.50
Period unsignedInt RW 
Unit: seconds. Time period which is used to regularly check the GRE connection state - after a connection loss.
Default value is 30 seconds.
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_WiFi.GRE.{i}.Stats. Object R
- 6.50
PacketsReceived unsignedInt R 
Unit: packets.
 - 6.50
BytesReceived unsignedInt R 
Unit: bytes.
 - 6.50
PacketsSent unsignedInt R 
Unit: packets.
 - 6.50
BytesSent unsignedInt R 
Unit: bytes.
 - 6.50
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_PCP. Object R
- 7.20
SupportedVersions unsignedInt[0:65535] R 
Comma-separated list.
 2 7.20
ClientNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_PCP.Client.{i}. Object R
- 7.20
Enable boolean R 
Current Status of PCP Client.
 1 7.20
ServerNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_PCP.Client.{i}.Server.{i}. Object R
- 7.20
Status string R 
The status of the PCP Server.
{{enum}} {{enum|Enabled}} {{enum|Disabled}}
Enumeration of:
- Disabled
- Enabled
 Disabled 7.20
ServerNameOrAddress string(256) R 
The FQDN or IP address of the PCP Server, assigned as described under {{param|Origin}}.
 - 7.20
ExternalIPAddress IPv4Address R 
The external IP address of the PCP-controlled device hosting this server.
 - 7.20
ServerNameOrIPv6Address string(256) R 
The FQDN or IPv6 address of the PCP Server, assigned as described under {{param|Origin}}.
 - 7.20
ExternalIPv6Address IPv6Address R 
The external IPv6 address of the PCP-controlled device hosting this server.
 - 7.20
PortRanges string RW 
Used to set the port ranges available for the user. Multiple port ranges could be specified using comma-seperation, eg.: "31200-31300,34500-34550"
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.X_AVM-DE_TWAMPReflector. Object RW 
This object contains parameters associated with the configuration that permits this interface to be used as Two-Way Active Measurement Protocol (TWAMP) reflector
 - 7.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enables or disables the TWAMP reflector.

Note: After a reboot of the CPE the default value (enabled = false) will be set
 0 7.20
Port unsignedInt[:65535] RW 
The port used to listen for the TWAMP test packets.
 862 7.20
MaximumTTL unsignedInt[1:255] RW 
The maximum TTL of a received packet that this TWAMP reflector will reflect to the TWAMP controller.
 255 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}. Object R 
Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
VoiceProfileNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R 
number of SIP accounts
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.Capabilities. Object R 
Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
MaxProfileCount unsignedInt R 
20 (MAX_SIP_PROVIDERS)
 - 6.20
MaxLineCount unsignedInt R 
20 (MAX_SIP_PROVIDERS)
 - 6.20
MaxSessionsPerLine unsignedInt R 
1
 - 6.20
MaxSessionCount unsignedInt R 
4
 - 6.20
SignalingProtocols string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "SIP/2.0".
Possible Patterns:
- SIP
- MGCP
- MGCP-NCS
- H\.248
- H\.323
- SIP/\d+\.\d+
- MGCP/\d+\.\d+
- MGCP-NCS/\d+\.\d+
- H\.248/\d+\.\d+
- H\.323/\d+\.\d+
- X_.+
 - 6.20
Regions string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "" (empty string)
 - 6.20
RTCP boolean R 
true , [ Receice yes - Send no ]
 - 6.20
SRTP boolean R 
true
 - 6.20
SRTPKeyingMethods string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "SDP"
Enumeration of:
- Null
- Static
- SDP
- IKE
 - 6.20
SRTPEncryptionKeySizes unsignedInt(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "128"
 - 6.20
RTPRedundancy boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
DSCPCoupled boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
EthernetTaggingCoupled boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
PSTNSoftSwitchOver boolean R 
true   (if Fallback then true)
 - 6.20
FaxT38 boolean R 
true
 - 6.20
FaxPassThrough boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
ModemPassThrough boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
ToneGeneration boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
RingGeneration boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
NumberingPlan boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
ButtonMap boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
VoicePortTests boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_UsePSTN boolean RW 
PSTN support
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ToS_SIP unsignedInt RW 
sip_prio (allowed values 0...63)
ToS value for SIP pack.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ToS_RTP unsignedInt RW 
rtp_prio (allowed values 0...63)
ToS value for RTP pack.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ToS_RTCP unsignedInt RW 
rtcp_prio (allowed values 0...63)
ToS value for RTCP pack.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_EthernetPriorityMark_SIP_Local unsignedInt[0:7] RW 
VLAN priority for outgoing SIP traffic for local (DECT or wired) telephones
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_EthernetPriorityMark_RTP_Local unsignedInt[0:7] RW 
VLAN priority for outgoing RTP traffic for local (DECT or wired) telephones
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_EthernetPriorityMark_SIP_LAN unsignedInt[0:7] RW 
VLAN priority for outgoing SIP traffic for LAN IP telephones
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_EthernetPriorityMark_RTP_LAN unsignedInt[0:7] RW 
VLAN priority for outgoing RTP  traffic for LAN IP  telephones
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_DialogLimit unsignedInt RW 
dialoglimit (allowed values 0...32)
DialogLimit over all lines
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_VoIP_Over_Mobile boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
use X_AVM-DE_MobileCapability
voip_over_mobile  (default = no)
If true allow VoIP via UMTS.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MobileCapability int RW 
Global setting for all configured VoIP UAs.  Defines which medias are to be used for VoIP. Allowed values are:
0: VoIP over Mobile not allowed (Default for all non-LTE devices)
1: VoIP allowed over all medias
2: VoIP allowed only via Mobile (UMTS, LTE)
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_RegisterSequenceTime unsignedInt RW 
Time interval in seconds between registation. Allowed values: 0 ... 30 (default=0)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ReduceDatarate boolean RW 
Reduce datarate during voip-session to optimize speech quality (default=1).
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_GUI_ReadOnly boolean RW 
If true WebGUI does not allow changes over all SIP accounts. Furthermore prevents adding and deleting SIP accounts.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_use_DQoS boolean RW 
DQoS over all lines. Only available for cable boxes.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_SupportedCodecs string R 
Returns commaseparated list of available codecs.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_AlternateCodecList string RW 
Read: Returns empty string if internal codec list is used or specific userdefined codesclist. Priority is in descending order. Write: Set a commasepareted codesc list  to use specific priority and codecs. First is highest priority. Codecs must be out of X_AVM-DE_SupportedCodecs. Can be overridden by user agent specific parameter X_AVM-DE_codec_override.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ISDNMode string RW 
0: extern S0 Mehrgeraete-anschluss / intern S0 powersave active;
1: extern S0 Mehrgeraete-anschluss / intern S0 powersave inactive;
2: extern S0 Anlagen-anschluss / intern powersave inactive
3: extern S0 Anlagenanschluss / intern: S0 P2P + DDI without trunknumber + powersave inactive/ 7583: 2x P2P + no Multi-S0
4: extern S0 Mehrgeraeteanschluss / intern: S0 P2MP + powersave inactive / 7583: 2x P2P + Multi-S0. Fits only for 7583, otherwise equal to 1
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.Capabilities.SIP. Object R 
SIPEndpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
Role string R 
"UserAgent"
Enumeration of:
- UserAgent
- BackToBackUserAgents
- OutboundProxy
 - 6.20
Extensions string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "INVITE","ACK", "OPTIONS", "CANCEL", "BYE","UPDATE","PRACK","INFO","SUBSCRIBE","NOTIFY", "REFER","MESSAGE"
 - 6.20
Transports string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. "UDP","TCP","TLS"
Enumeration of:
- UDP
- TCP
- TLS
 - 6.20
URISchemes string(256) R 
Comma-separated list. ""
 - 6.20
EventSubscription boolean R 
false / if  true then instaciate EventSubscribe obj)
 - 6.20
ResponseMap boolean R 
false
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_RandomSIPPortEnable boolean RW 
If true, use a random generated portnumber for the UserAgentPort of the portrange, defined in X_AVM-DE_RandomSIPPortRange.
 false 7.50
X_AVM-DE_RandomSIPPortRange string RW 
UserAgentPort is random calculated from this portrange. Syntax: "<srcport>-<destport>". The calculated port is returned in UserAgentPort.
 20000-30000 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.Capabilities.Codecs.{i}. Object R 
At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: EntryID, Codec, BitRate. for each codec is instanciated an object: "G.711MuLaw", "G.711.ALaw", "G.726", "G.729", "iLBC"
Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
EntryID unsignedInt[1:] R 
unique Identifier, (1...n Codecs)
 - 6.20
Codec string(64) R 
s.o.
Enumeration of:
- G.711MuLaw
- G.711ALaw
- G.726
- G.729
- G.729a
- G.729e
- G.728
- G.723.1
- G.722
- G.722.1
- G.722.2
- GSM-FR
- GSM-HR
- GSM-EFR
- iLBC
 - 6.20
BitRate unsignedInt R 
Unit: bits per second. G.711.*: 64000, G.726:32000, G.729:8000, iLBC:13333
 - 6.20
PacketizationPeriod string(64) R 
Comma-separated list. 20
 - 6.20
SilenceSuppression boolean R 
true
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Name. for each SIP account must be VoiceProfile object present
Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
Enable string RW 
Enumeration of:
- Disabled
- Quiescent (OPTIONAL)
- Enabled
 Disabled 6.20
Reset boolean RW
- 6.20
NumberOfLines unsignedInt R 
1
 0 6.20
Name string(64) RW 
initial empty string
 6.20
SignalingProtocol string R 
"SIP/2.0" The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the SignalingProtocols parameter.
 6.20
MaxSessions unsignedInt R 
4
 0 6.20
DTMFMethod string(64) RW 
InBand, RFC2833, SIPInfo
Enumeration of:
- InBand
- RFC2833
- SIPInfo (Applicable only if the value of {{param|SignalingProtocol}} is SIP.)
 InBand 6.20
DTMFMethodG711 string(64) RW 
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
Enumeration of:
- InBand
- RFC2833
- SIPInfo (Applicable only if the value of {{param|SignalingProtocol}} is SIP.)
-
6.20
STUNServer string(256) RW
6.20
PSTNFailOver boolean RW 
optional parameter, acc. PSTN Fallback
 false 6.20
X_AVM-DE_BackupConnectionFailOver boolean RW 
Indicates if in case of voip connection loss or SIP register fail is switched over to the backup connection. See object X_AVM-DE_BackupConnection.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_route_always_over_internet boolean RW 
If true voip-traffic is always routed over internet connection and not over separate voip connection.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CallingFeatures int RW 
Possible value: 0=standard, 1=cable special
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_GUI_ReadOnly boolean RW 
Write Protection. If true WebGUI does not allow changes of this account. Not available for all AVM CPEs.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.ServiceProviderInfo. Object R
- 6.20
Name string(256) RW
6.20
URL string(256) RW 
not retained across reboots
 6.20
ContactPhoneNumber string(32) RW 
not retained across reboots
 6.20
EmailAddress string(256) RW 
not retained across reboots
 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.SIP. Object R 
SIPEndpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
ProxyServer string(256) RW
6.20
ProxyServerPort unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
<port> (def.5060)
 5060 6.20
ProxyServerTransport string RW 
Allows you to force a specific transport_type protocol for this voice profile.
If this parameter has not been configured, the transport_type will be negotiated automatically.

Note: The currently used transport_type can be read with 'X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed'
Enumeration of:
- UDP
- TCP
- TLS
- Unspecified (negotiated dynamically)
 Unspecified 6.20
RegistrarServer string(256) RW
6.20
RegistrarServerPort unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
<port> (def. 5060)
 5060 6.20
RegistrarServerTransport string RW 
Allows you to force a specific transport_type protocol for this voice profile.
If this parameter has not been configured, the transport_type will be negotiated automatically.

Note: The currently used transport_type can be read with 'X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed'
Enumeration of:
- UDP
- TCP
- TLS
- Unspecified (negotiated dynamically)
 Unspecified 6.20
UserAgentDomain string(256) R 
external IP-Address (not writable yet)
 6.20
UserAgentPort unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
Port used for incoming call control signaling.If X_AVM-DE_RandomSIPPortEnable is true a random generated port of the given portrange in X_AVM-DE_RandomSIPPortRange is used and returned.
 5060 6.20
UserAgentTransport string RW 
Allows you to force a specific transport_type protocol for this voice profile.
If this parameter has not been configured, the transport_type will be negotiated automatically.

Note: The currently used transport_type can be read with 'X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed'
Enumeration of:
- UDP
- TCP
- TLS
- Unspecified (negotiated dynamically)
 Unspecified 6.20
OutboundProxy string(256) RW
6.20
OutboundProxyPort unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
<port>
 5060 6.20
Organization string(256) RW 
supported, but not restored in any cfg
 6.20
RegistrationPeriod unsignedInt[1:] RW 
Unit: seconds. (def. 1800)
 - 6.20
RegisterExpires unsignedInt[1:] RW 
Unit: seconds. (def. 1800)
 - 6.20
UseCodecPriorityInSDPResponse boolean R 
(not writable yet)
 false 6.20
X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed string R 
The currently used transport_type of the voice profile.

Note:
'VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.SIP.RegistrarServerTransport' allows you to force a specific transport_type protocol.
If this parameter has not been configured, the transport_type will be negotiated automatically
Enumeration of:
- UDP
- TCP
- TLS
- - (not connected)
 - 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.RTP. Object R 
Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
LocalPortMin unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
default 7078
 0 6.20
LocalPortMax unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
range currently rtpport_start+17
 0 6.20
DSCPMark unsignedInt[0:63] RW 
Implementation status: functionality is not supported
 - 6.20
TelephoneEventPayloadType unsignedInt[0:128] R 
returns always "101", negociable according RFC2833
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_tx_packetsize_in_ms unsignedInt RW 
Unit: ms. allowed values 0...30
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Packetization unsignedInt RW 
Allowed values are 0,1, 2:
0 = packetization_fixed: Use of X_AVM-DE_tx_packetsize_in_ms for the packetsize.
1 = packetization_auto: Always use ptimeof the opposite party. If the opposite party does not transfer a ptime F!OS will use a default size of 20ms.
2 = packetization_b_side: If the opposite party does not transfer a ptime F!OS will use a default size of 20ms. If the opposite party tranfers a ptime F!OS will use the ptimeof the called party. If F!OS is the called party, F!OS will use X_AVM-DE_tx_packetsize_in_ms.
 0 7.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.RTP.RTCP. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
cannot be disabled
 - 6.20
TxRepeatInterval unsignedInt[1:] RW 
Unit: milliseconds. 5000ms, cannot be changed
 - 6.20
LocalCName string(64) RW
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_rtcp_xr_media_attribute boolean RW 
SDP attribute fuer RTCP-XR (a=rtcp-xr:voip-metrics).
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ptime_a_attribute string RW 
ok;bool. SDP ptime attribute. (a=ptime:XX). See RFC4566.
Implementation status:  ptime_a_attribute
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_tones_and_announcements_for_service boolean RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.RTP.SRTP. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
KeyingMethods string(256) RW 
Comma-separated list. only "SDP" supported The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the SRTPKeyingMethods parameter.
 - 6.20
EncryptionKeySizes string(256) RW 
Comma-separated list. only "128" supported The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the SRTPEncryptionKeySizes parameter.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.FaxT38. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
T38 will be activated for all VoiceProfiles
 - 6.20
BitRate unsignedInt[33600:33600] RW 
14400
Read only
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.Line.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: DirectoryNumber. Endpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
Enable string RW 
Enumeration of:
- Disabled
- Quiescent (OPTIONAL)
- Enabled
 Disabled 6.20
DirectoryNumber string(32) RW
6.20
Status string R 
returns "Up" or "Error"
Enumeration of:
- Up
- Initializing
- Registering
- Unregistering
- Error
- Testing
- Quiescent
- Disabled
 Disabled 6.20
CallState string R 
returns "Idle" or "InCall"
Enumeration of:
- Idle
- Calling
- Ringing
- Connecting
- InCall
- Hold
- Disconnecting
 Idle 6.20
PhyReferenceList string(32) RW 
Comma-separated list. Mapping for incoming calls of this line to the physical ports.  (kommasep. List, for ex.: "P0,P1,D0,I0,L0"). DECT fones and IP fones must be registered by the user to F!Box before assigning VoIP lines to them.
Note: Physical Port Assignment: Setting of this parameter must be performed in a separate SetParameterValue Request after provisioning all VoIP Accounts.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_PhyOutgoingNumberPort string RW 
Mapping for outgoing calls of this line to the phys. ports (kommasep. List, for ex. "P0,P1,I0,D0,L0"). For optimization reasons an outgoing number is automatically assigned as incoming number for this port. DECT fones and IP fones must be registered to F!Box by the user before assigning VoIP lines to them.
Note: Physical Port Assignment: Setting of this parameter must be performed in a separate SetParameterValue Request after provisioning all VoIP Accounts.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.Line.{i}.SIP. Object R 
SIPEndpoint-Profile
 - 6.20
AuthUserName string(128) RW
6.20
AuthPassword string(128) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 6.20
URI string(389) RW
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CLIRType unsignedInt RW 
: CLIR type allowed values are:
clir_none  = 0
clir_displayname = 1
clir_display_and_username = 2
clir_aol = 3
clir_privacy = 4
clir_rfc3325 = 5
clir_privacy_id = 6
clir_star31 = 7
clir_star31star = 8
clir_toi = 9
clir_star31hash = 10
clir_star67star = 11
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CLIP_ns unsignedInt RW 
CLIP no screening, allowed values are:
0=clipns_off, 1=clipns_asserted_identity, 2=clipns_preferred_identity
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CLIP_use_From_DisplayName boolean RW 
CLIP: use displayname from "From"-SIP header, default = yes
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_OKZ string RW 
Area code without leading AreaCode Prefix ('0').
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_OKZPrefix string RW 
Only available in OEM=avme. Override existing AreaCode prefix.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_CountryCode string RW 
Country Code without leading '0'.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_PAccessNetworkInfo string RW 
(maxLen=1024), RFC3455 Support
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_UseAuthUsername boolean RW 
use internet phone number for Auth.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_AuthName string RW 
Authname write access provider specific.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_g726_via_rfc3551_supported boolean RW 
use G726 via RFC3551
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_no_g726_32_offer_with_pt2 boolean RW 
setting for PayloadType 2 for Codec G.726-32
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_g726_fixed_ptime30 boolean RW 
fix ptime to 30ms for G726 codecs
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_dtmf_inband_on_g711g722 boolean RW 
use dtmf_inband for codecs G711 and G722
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_codec_override string RW 
Read: Returns empty string if internal codec list is used or specific userdefined codeclist. Priority is in descending order.
Write: Set a commaseparetad codec list to use specific priority and codecs for this user agent. First is highest priority.
Codecs must be out of X_AVMDE_SupportedCodecs.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_enable_3xx boolean RW 
setting for accepting 3xx replys
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_t38_reinvite_from_remote boolean RW
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_use_488_for_no_t38 boolean RW 
Use 488 if T.38 is not enabled
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_t38_Version0 boolean RW 
Send T.38 Fax Version '0' in offer.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CCBS_enable boolean RW 
Completion of Calls to Busy Subscriber
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_use_rport boolean RW 
Provider supports rport (RFC 3581)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_SessionTimer_supported boolean RW 
Provider supports Session Timer (RFC 4028)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ignore_received_header boolean RW
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MWI_supported boolean RW 
Provider supports MWI (RFC 3482)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MWI_memorize boolean RW 
Allow NOTIFYs outside of dialog.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_NAT_aware boolean RW 
Provider is NAT aware
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_only_one_dialog boolean RW 
Provider supports only 1 dialog
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_REGISTER_fetch_unsupported boolean RW 
Provider does not support REGISTER-fetch
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_VoIP_Over_Mobile boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
use X_AVM-DE_MobileCapability --
Route VoIP over mobile (UMTS, LTE) connection
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MobileCapability int RW 
Defines which medias are to be used for this UA. 
This setting depends also on the global setting in VoiceService.Capabilities.X_AVM-DE_MobileCapability.
Allowed values are:
0: VoIP over Mobile not allowed for this UA
1: VoIP allowed over all medias for this UA (Default)
2: VoIP allowed only via Mobile (UMTS, LTE) for this UA
 - 7.10
X_AVM-DE_reject_REFER boolean RW 
reject REFER without authentication
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_allow_call_only_from_registrar boolean RW 
Allow incoming calls only from registrar. (SPIT)
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_INVITE_without_Registration boolean RW 
allow Invite without being registered
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Disabled_AutoRegistration boolean RW 
Do not register this account automatically.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_alias_head_number string RW 
Alias Support. Set here head number. Flag X_AVM-DE_Disabled_AutoRegistration must be set to 'true'.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_P_Asserted_Identity_Header boolean RW 
support for RFC3325
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_sipping_enable boolean RW 
enable sipping
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_sipping_interval unsignedInt RW 
sipping interval in seconds. Allowed values 0...300 sec.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_sip_options_ping_interval string RW 
Interval in seconds. Availability check of the registrar via SIP OPTIONS: In case of 503 (with Retry-After) or timeout a reregistration to the next registrar is triggered. Interval should not be less than 60 sec. 0 = feature is deactivated.
 0 7.50
X_AVM-DE_register_failwait unsignedInt RW 
Retrytime in seconds after a failed Register. Allowed values: 0 ...86400, 0 = dyn. Retrys (default).
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_register_failwaitmax unsignedInt RW 
RetryMaxtime in seconds after a failed Register. Allowed values: 1 ...86400.(default=1800))
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_register_failover_fallback boolean RW 
If enabled reachability of first registrar/SBC is checked before ReRegister.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_outboundproxy_without_route_header boolean RW
- 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Factory_3PTY_URI string RW 
IMS: 3PTY URI
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Local_Speech_disable boolean RW 
: IMS: disable local speech.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_ProtocolPrefer string RW 
Specifies protocol to use:
uInt:  0 = ipv4only, 1 = ipv6only, 2=ipv4prefer, 3=ipv6prefer
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_use_DQoS boolean RW 
DQoS for this line. Only available for cable boxes.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_RFC6947_altc_attribute boolean RW 
See RFC6947, SDP, Alternate Connectivity (ALTC) Attribute
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_send_extended_sip_client_info boolean RW 
Send extended sip client information or not.
 - 6.90
X_AVM-DE_sip_instance boolean RW 
See RFC5626. Use of "sip.instance" media feature tag which appears as a "+sip.instance" Contact header field parameter.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_Loopback_client_username_FOS string RW 
Username of Caller (TraceSim). Speech data is looped within FRITZ!OS.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_Loopback_client_username_pass_through string RW 
Username of Caller (TraceSim). Speech data is passed to end-device.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_directLoopback_client_username string RW 
Username of Caller (TraceSim). Speech data is looped back before they pass encoder/decoder.
 - 6.50
X_AVM-DE_International_Number_Format boolean RW 
If set to true will use International Calling Number Format.
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_TLS_Version string R 
If (X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed != TLS), 'noTLS' is returned.
If (X_AVM-DE_TransportUsed == TLS), the negotiated tls_version is returned (eg. '1.2', '1.1')
 - 7.00
X_AVM-DE_Crypto_AVP_Mode int RW 
X_AVM-DE_crypto_AVP_Mode defines which media descriptions should be included in SDP.
Allowed values:
0 (crypto_mode_avp_only): The SRTP key is included in the RTP/AVP media protocol. (default)
1 (crypto_mode_avp_and_savp):  There are 2 media lines: RTP/AVP and RTP/SAVP. The SRTP key is included in the RTP/SAVP media protocol.
2 (crypto_mode_savp_only): The SRTP key is included in the RTP/SAVP media protocol.
 - 7.20
X_AVM-DE_CallDeflection boolean RW 
Provider supports Call Deflection/Partial Rerouting via '302 Moved Temporarily'
 false 7.20
X_AVM-DE_Answer_only_one_codec boolean RW 
true: Use only the first matching codec from remote SDP offer when negotiating RTP. Note: mime type "telephone-event/8000/1" (RFC 4733) is no codec.
false: Use all matching codecs from remote SDP offer when negotiating RTP
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_Use_IMS_retry boolean RW 
true: Use IMS retry mechanism in failure cases.
false: Use ordinary registration routine.
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_Reg_event_policy unsignedInt[0:2] RW 
Enable/Disable subscription for 'reg' events (RFC 3680)

0: SUBSCRIBE if 'reg' is in "Allow-Events" header of 200 (OK).
1: SUBSCRIBE for 'reg' events.
2: Do not SUBSCRIBE for 'reg' events.
 0 7.25
X_AVM-DE_Reject_anonymous_call_with_433 boolean RW 
true: Reject anonymous calls (CLIR) via SIP code 433 (RFC 5079)
false: Reject anonymous calls (CLIR) via SIP code 480 (RFC 3261)
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_RTP_event_with_constant_packetrate boolean RW 
true: Drop RTP packets to keep packetrate constant during "telephone-event" (RFC 4733)
false: Do not drop RTP packets during "telephone-event" (RFC 4733)
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_INVITE_with_displayname boolean RW 
true: Send displayname, depending on X_AVM-DE_CLIRType and ddi_type
false: Do not send displayname
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_AnnexB_in_SDP boolean RW 
true: implicit "annexb=no" in SDP for G.729
false: explicit "annexb=no" in SDP for G.729
 false 7.25
X_AVM-DE_require_mediasec boolean RW 
If enabled the header "Require: mediasec" and "Proxy-Require: mediasec" are set for REGISTER and INVITE requests for encrypted telephony.
 false 8.10
X_AVM-DE_failover_5xx_with_retry_after boolean RW 
If enabled, failover on SIP response 503/504 for INVITE/REGISTER requires "Retry-After" header to be present. 
If disabled, header "Retry-After" must not be present for failover to take place. Not applicable when ims_client is set.
 true 7.50
X_AVM-DE_failover_5xx_register_after_invite boolean RW 
If enabled, failover any on 503/504 for INVITE requests is followed by a new REGISTER.
If disabled, a new INVITE is directly sent after failover.
 false 7.50
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.VoiceProfile.{i}.Line.{i}.SIP.X_AVM-DE_DDI. Object R
- 6.20
Central string RW 
Central Number.
 - 6.20
ExtensionLength unsignedInt RW 
Length of Extension Numbers.
 - 6.20
Trunk string RW 
Trunk Number
 - 6.20
ddi_type unsignedInt RW 
0 = ddi_none
1 = ddi_displayname
2 = ddi_from
3 = ddi_from_with_displayname
4 = ddi_prefered_identity
 - 6.20
Add_Plusprefix boolean RW 
If set to true a '+' will be added into the 'From'-Field. ddi_type and X_AVM-DE_International_Number_Format must be set as well.
 - 7.00

 - 6.20
