InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.PhyInterface.{i}. Object R 
At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: InterfaceID, PhyPort. there is 1 object for each phys. port
 - 6.20
PhyPort string(2) R 
can be:
P0, P1, P2 = analog ports
I0 = S0 bus
D0 ... D5 = DECT
L0 ...  L9 = LAN/WLAN IP Fon
F0 = internal Fax
T0 ... T4 = internal Answering Machine
 - 6.20
InterfaceID unsignedInt[1:] R 
0 ... 25
 - 6.20
Description string(32) R 
can be:
"a/b", "ISDN S0", "DECT", "LAN/WLAN  IP Fon", "internal Fax", "Fon Answering Machine"
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_FriendlyName string RW 
max length 31 characters. User readable friendly name of the port.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_BusyOnBusy boolean R 
BusyOnBusy configured on this port
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MessageWaitIndication boolean R 
MWI configured on this port
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_RingBlock boolean R 
RingBlock configured on this port
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_CallWaiting boolean R 
indicates if Call Waiting has been configured on this port
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_SIPClientRegistration_from_Internet boolean RW 
indicates if Registration from Internet is allowed . Only applicable for LAN/WLAN IP phones.
 - 6.20
X_AVM-DE_MSNList string RW 
max. length 512. Commaseparated list of assigned phone numbers (MSN's) to this physical interface. Includes VoIP MSN's, PSTN MSN's and GSM-MSN.
The commaseparated list defines the outgoing number und the incoming numbers for this physical interface. Keywords: "out:<MSN>" "in:<MSN>" "in:all"
Example: following MSNs are configured: 11111, 22222, 0493039976
Example 1 for P0: Outgoing MSN is 11111, analog phone will ring on all incoming calls:
"out:11111, in:all" 
Example 2 for D0: Outgoing MSN is 22222, Dect phone will ring on incoming calls on 22222, 33333 and 0493039976:
"out:22222, in:22222, in:33333, in:0493039976"
Example 3 for T0: Answering Machine listens on MSN 11111: "in:11111" .
No outgoing numbers must be defined for PhyInterface "TAM" and "FAX".
S0 Bus: as default all MSNs are incoming.
Note:
The MSNs must be provisioned before assigning them to PhyInterfaces.
PhyInterface assigned MSNs must match exactly the configured MSNs.
DECT fones and IP fones must be registered by the user to F!Box before assigning MSN's to them.
Setting of this parameter must be performed in a
separate SetParameterValue Request after
provisioning all VoIP Accounts.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_BackupConnection. Object R 
Specifies whether or not the CPE SHOULD fail over to the internet connection service, if available, on loss of connectivity of the VoIP connection or SIP registration failure. This parameter is appropriate only in implementations in which a separate VoIP connection is used.
 - 6.20
BackupEnable boolean RW
- 6.20
BackupWait unsignedInt RW 
Time in seconds until VoIP service fails over to the internet connection. Default: 60 seconds.
 - 6.20
BackupFirstCheck unsignedInt RW 
Check Time interval  in seconds until switching back from backup state to regular operation state.  This value is doubled after each check until BackupMaxCheck. Default start check interval: 90 seconds.
 - 6.20
BackupMaxCheck unsignedInt RW 
The maximum Check Time interval  in seconds until switching back from backup state to regular operation state. Default max check interval: 300 seconds.
 - 6.20
BackupRandom unsignedInt RW 
A random value is calculated between 0 and BackupRandom. It will be added to all backup timers. Default: 30 seconds.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ISDN. Object R 
ISDN support for max. 10 MSN's
 - 6.20
ISDNLineNumberOfEntries string R 
10 Line objects are preinstanciated
 - 6.20
CountryCode string RW 
without leading '0'
 - 6.20
AreaCode string RW 
without leading '0'
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_ISDN.Line.{i}. Object R
- 6.20
MSN string RW 
ISDN MSN without areacode
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_AdvancedSettings. Object R 
AVM vendor specific object. Corresponds with equivalent GUI settings.
 - 6.20
DECTEnabled boolean R
- 6.20
ForceGAPCompatibility boolean R
- 6.20
CallDiversionActive boolean R
- 6.20
VoiceCoding string RW 
Allowed values: 
"auto" (automatic selection of coding), 
"fixed" ( fixed line quality),
"compressed" (use always coding with), 
"autocompressed" (automatic use of compressed audio codec)
 - 6.20
BlockingRules string R 
User readable string that indicates the type of outgoing calls to be blocked. (special calling numbers, national calls, mobile calls ...)
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_OnTel. Object R 
AVM vendor specific object: Online Phonebook. If more than 1 Online Phonebook is supported use object "..X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.i.". Further online phonebooks can be added by method "AddObject" on object "OnTel"
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
Name string RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 6.20
Status string R 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
LastConnect string R 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
Url string RW 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
ServiceId string RW 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
Username string RW 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
Password string RW 
DEPRECATED
Only for compatibility reasons with former firmware versions. This parameter is equivalant to X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.1.parametername
This parameter is deprecated and supported only til TR-069 Version 15.05.02
 - 6.20
OnTelNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.VoiceService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_OnTel.OnTel.{i}. Object RW 
max. 10 online phonebooks supported
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
Status string R
- 6.20
LastConnect string R
- 6.20
Url string RW
- 6.20
ServiceId string RW
- 6.20
Username string RW
- 6.20
Password string RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
returns always true
 - 6.20
PhysicalMediumNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
LogicalVolumeNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
UserAccountNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.Capabilities. Object R
- 6.20
FTPCapable boolean R 
FTP supported
 - 6.20
SFTPCapable boolean R 
SFTP not supported
 - 6.20
HTTPCapable boolean R 
HTTP not supported
 - 6.20
HTTPSCapable boolean R 
HTTPS not supported
 - 6.20
HTTPWritable boolean R 
HTTPWritable not supported
 - 6.20
SupportedNetworkProtocols string R 
Comma-separated list. "SMB"
Enumeration of:
- SMB
- NFS
- AFP
 - 6.20
SupportedFileSystemTypes string R 
Comma-separated list. "FAT16,FAT32,NTFS"
Enumeration of:
- FAT16
- FAT32
- NTFS
- HFS
- HFS+
- HSFJ
- ext2
- ext3
- XFS
- REISER
 - 6.20
VolumeEncryptionCapable boolean R 
VolumeEncryption not supported
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.NetInfo. Object R
- 6.20
HostName string(64) RW
- 6.20
DomainName string(255) RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.UserAccount.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Username.
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
false 6.20
Username string(64) RW
6.20
Password string(64) RW 
When read, this parameter returns an empty string, regardless of the actual value.
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.NetworkServer. Object R
- 6.20
AFPEnable boolean RW 
AFP not supported, cannot be enabled
 - 6.20
NFSEnable boolean RW 
NFS not supported, cannot be enabled
 - 6.20
SMBEnable boolean RW
- 6.20
NetworkProtocolAuthReq boolean RW 
cannot be disabled
 - 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.FTPServer. Object R
- 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
Status string R 
"Enabled", "Disabled", "Error"
Enumeration of:
- Enabled
- Disabled
- Error
 - 6.20
MaxNumUsers unsignedInt[1:32] RW 
default is 32
 - 6.20
IdleTime unsignedInt[0:600] RW 
Unit: seconds. default is 120
 - 6.20
PortNumber unsignedInt[0:65535] RW 
default is 21
 21 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.FTPServer.AnonymousUser. Object R 
Anonymous User not supported
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
StartingFolder string(256) RW 
The value MUST be the path name of a table row.
 - 6.20
ReadOnlyAccess boolean RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.HTTPServer. Object R 
HTTP not yet supported
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW 
Enable = true not supported
 - 6.20
Status string R 
Enumeration of:
- Enabled
- Disabled
- Error
 - 6.20
MaxNumUsers unsignedInt[1:32] RW
- 6.20
IdleTime unsignedInt[0:600] RW 
Unit: seconds.
 - 6.20
HTTPWritingEnabled boolean R
- 6.20
PortNumber unsignedInt[0:65535] RW
80 6.20
AuthenticationReq boolean RW
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.PhysicalMedium.{i}. Object R 
At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Name, Vendor, Model, SerialNumber. Details see Spec. TR-140
 - 6.20
Capacity unsignedInt R 
Unit: MB.
 - 6.20
ConnectionType string R 
Enumeration of:
- USB 1.1
- USB 2.0
- IEEE1394
- IEEE1394b
- IDE
- EIDE
- ATA/33
- ATA/66
- ATA/100
- ATA/133
- SATA/150
- SATA/300
- SCSI-1
- Fast SCSI
- Fast-Wide SCSI
- Ultra SCSI
- Ultra Wide SCSI
- Ultra2 SCSI
- Ultra2 Wide SCSI
- Ultra3 SCSI
- Ultra-320 SCSI
- Ultra-640 SCSI
- SSA
- SSA-40
- Fibre Channel
 - 6.20
FirmwareVersion string(64) R
- 6.20
HotSwappable boolean R 
returns false
 - 6.20
Model string(128) R
- 6.20
Name string(64) RW
- 6.20
Removable boolean R 
returns true
 - 6.20
SerialNumber string(64) R
- 6.20
SMARTCapable boolean R
- 6.20
Status string R 
Enumeration of:
- Online
- Standby
- Offline
 - 6.20
Vendor string(64) R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.LogicalVolume.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Name. Details see Spec. TR-140
 - 6.20
Name string(64) RW
6.20
Status string R 
Enumeration of:
- Offline
- Online
- Error
 Offline 6.20
Enable boolean RW
false 6.20
PhysicalReference string(256) RW 
The value MUST be the path name of a table row.
 - 6.20
FileSystem string R 
The value MUST be a member of the list reported by the SupportedFileSystemTypes parameter.
 - 6.20
Capacity unsignedInt RW 
Unit: MB.
 - 6.20
UsedSpace unsignedInt R 
Unit: MB.
 - 6.20
Encrypted boolean R
- 6.20
FolderNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.LogicalVolume.{i}.Folder.{i}. Object RW 
At most one enabled entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: Name. Details see Spec. TR-140
 - 6.20
Name string(64) RW
6.20
Enable boolean RW
false 6.20
UserAccountAccess unsignedInt[0:3] RW
2 6.20
UserAccessNumberOfEntries unsignedInt R
- 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.LogicalVolume.{i}.Folder.{i}.UserAccess.{i}. Object RW 
At most one entry in this table can exist with all the same values for: UserReference. Details see Spec. TR-140
 - 6.20
UserReference string(256) RW 
The value MUST be the path name of a table row.
 6.20
Permissions unsignedInt[0:7] RW
7 6.20
InternetGatewayDevice.Services.StorageService.{i}.X_AVM-DE_WebDAVClient.{i}. Object RW 
needs AVM TR-069 Stack Version >= 12.03.01
 - 6.20
Enable boolean RW
- 6.20
HostURL string RW
- 6.20
Username string RW
- 6.20
Password string RW
- 6.20
MountpointName string RW 
needs AVM TR-069 Stack Version >=13.04.02
 - 6.20
Data Type Base Type Description
Alias string(64) 
A non-volatile handle used to reference this instance. Alias provides a mechanism for an ACS to label this instance for future reference.
If the CPE supports the Alias-based Addressing feature as defined in [3.6.1/TR-069a4] and described in [Appendix II/TR-069a4], the following mandatory constraints MUST be enforced:
*Its value MUST NOT be empty.
*Its value MUST start with a letter.
*If its value is not assigned by the ACS, it MUST start with a "cpe-" prefix.
*The CPE MUST NOT change the parameter value.
Dbm1000 int 
The value is measured in ''dBm/1000'', i.e. the value divided by 1000 is dB relative to 1 mW.  For example, -12345 means -12.345 dBm, 0 means 0 dBm (1 mW) and 12345 means 12.345 dBm.
IEEE_EUI64 string(23) 
The IEEE EUI 64-bit identifier as defined in [EUI64]. The IEEE defined 64-bit extended unique identifier (EUI-64) is a concatenation of:
* The 24-bit (OUI-24) or 36-bit (OUI-36) company_id value assigned by the IEEE Registration Authority (IEEE-RA), and
* The extension identifier (40 bits for OUI-24 or 28 bits for OUI-36) assigned by the organization with that company_id assignment.
Possible Patterns:
- (empty)
- ([0-9A-Fa-f][0-9A-Fa-f]:){7}([0-9A-Fa-f][0-9A-Fa-f])
IPAddress string(45) 
IP address, i.e. IPv4 address (or IPv4 subnet mask) or IPv6 address.
All IPv4 addresses and subnet masks MUST be represented as strings in IPv4 dotted-decimal notation.  Here are some examples of valid IPv4 address textual representations:
* 216.52.29.100
* 192.168.1.254
All IPv6 addresses MUST be represented using any of the 3 standard textual representations defined in [RFC4291] Sections 2.2.1, 2.2.2 and 2.2.3.  Both lower-case and upper-case letters can be used, but use of lower-case letters is RECOMMENDED.  Here are some examples of valid IPv6 address textual representations:
* 1080:0:0:800:ba98:3210:11aa:12dd
* 1080::800:ba98:3210:11aa:12dd
* 0:0:0:0:0:0:13.1.68.3
IPv6 addresses MUST NOT include zone identifiers. Zone identifiers are discussed in [Section 6/RFC4007].
Unspecified or inapplicable addresses (or IPv4 subnet masks) MUST be represented as empty strings unless otherwise specified by the parameter definition.
IPPrefix string(49) 
IPv4 or IPv6 routing prefix in Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) notation [RFC4632]. This is specified as an IP address followed by an appended "/n" suffix, where ''n'' (the prefix size) is an integer in the range 0-32 (for IPv4) or 0-128 (for IPv6) that indicates the number of (leftmost) '1' bits of the routing prefix. 
* IPv4 example: 192.168.1.0/24
* IPv6 example: 2001:edff:fe6a:f76::/64
If the IP address part is unspecified or inapplicable, it MUST be empty unless otherwise specified by the parameter definition.  In this case the IP prefix will be of the form "/n".
If the entire IP prefix is unspecified or inapplicable, it MUST be empty unless otherwise specified by the parameter definition.
IPv4Address IPAddress(15) 
IPv4 address (or subnet mask).
Can be any IPv4 address that is permitted by the ''IPAddress'' data type.
IPv4Prefix IPPrefix(18) 
IPv4 address prefix. 
Can be any IPv4 prefix that is permitted by the ''IPPrefix'' data type.
IPv6Address IPAddress 
IPv6 address.
Can be any IPv6 address that is permitted by the ''IPAddress'' data type.
IPv6Prefix IPPrefix 
IPv6 address prefix.
Can be any IPv6 prefix that is permitted by the ''IPPrefix'' data type.
MACAddress string(17) 
All MAC addresses are represented as strings of 12 hexadecimal digits (digits 0-9, letters A-F or a-f) displayed as six pairs of digits separated by colons.  Unspecified or inapplicable MAC addresses MUST be represented as empty strings unless otherwise specified by the parameter definition.
Possible Patterns:
- (empty)
- ([0-9A-Fa-f][0-9A-Fa-f]:){5}([0-9A-Fa-f][0-9A-Fa-f])
StatsCounter32 unsignedInt 
A 32-bit statistics parameter, e.g. a byte counter.
This data type SHOULD NOT be used for statistics parameters whose values might become greater than the maximum value that can be represented as an ''unsignedInt'' (i.e. 0xffffffff, referred to below as ''maxval''). ''StatsCounter64'' SHOULD be used for such parameters.
The value ''maxval'' indicates that no data is available for this parameter.  In the unlikely event that the actual value of the statistic is ''maxval'', the CPE SHOULD return ''maxval - 1''.
The actual value of the statistic might be greater than ''maxval''. Such values SHOULD wrap around through zero.
The term ''packet'' is to be interpreted as the transmission unit appropriate to the protocol layer in question, e.g. an IP packet or an Ethernet frame.
StatsCounter64 unsignedLong 
A 64-bit statistics parameter, e.g. a byte counter.
This data type SHOULD be used for all statistics parameters whose values might become greater than the maximum value that can be represented as an ''unsignedInt''.
The maximum value that can be represented as an ''unsignedLong'' (i.e. 0xffffffffffffffff) indicates that no data is available for this parameter.
The term ''packet'' is to be interpreted as the transmission unit appropriate to the protocol layer in question, e.g. an IP packet or an Ethernet frame.
UUID string(36:36) 
Universally Unique Identifier.  See [RFC4122].
Possible Patterns:
- [A-Fa-f0-9]{8}-[A-Fa-f0-9]{4}-[A-Fa-f0-9]{4}-[A-Fa-f0-9]{4}-[A-Fa-f0-9]{12}
ZigBeeNetworkAddress string(4) 
The ZigBee 16-bit network address (NWK) as defined in [ZigBee2007]. The address is assigned to a device by the network layer and used by the network layer for routing messages between devices.
Possible Patterns:
- (empty)
- ([0-9A-Fa-f]){4}
[3GPP-TS.23.107] 3GPP TS 23.107, Quality of Service (QoS) concept and architecture, 3GPP SA WG2
[3GPP-TS.25.171] 3GPP TS 25.171, Requirements for support of Assisted Global Positioning System (A-GPS), 3GPP RAN WG4
[3GPP-TS.32.582] 3GPP TS 32.582, Telecommunications management; Home Node B (HNB) Operations, Administration, Maintenance and Provisioning (OAM&P); Information model for Type 1 interface HNB to HNB Management System (HMS), 3GPP SA WG5
[3GPP-TS.32.584] 3GPP TS 32.584, Telecommunications management; Home Node B (HNB) Operations, Administration, Maintenance and Provisioning (OAM&P); XML definitions for Type 1 interface HNB to HNB Management Systems (HMS), 3GPP SA WG5
[3GPP-TS.32.592] 3GPP TS 32.592, Telecommunications management; Home enhanced Node B (HeNB) Operations, Administration, Maintenance and Provisioning (OAM&P); Information model for Type 1 interface HeNB to HeNB Management System (HeMS), 3GPP SA WG5
[3GPP2-S.S0132] S.S0132-0 v1.0, Femtocell Security Framework, 3GPP2, January 2010
[3GPP2-X.S0011] X.S0011-005-C, cdma2000 Wireless IP Network Standard: Accounting Services and 3GPP2 RADIUS VSAs, 3GPP2, August 2003
[802.1D-2004] IEEE Std 802.1D-2004, Media Access Control (MAC) Bridges, IEEE, 2004
[802.1Q-2005] IEEE Std 802.1Q-2005, Virtual Bridged Local Area Networks, IEEE, 2006
[BLUE] Blue, A New Class of Active Queue Management Algorithms
[DLNA-NDIG] DLNA Networked Device Interoperability Guidelines, DLNA Networked Device Interoperability Guidelines, Volume 2: Media Format Profiles., DLNA, October 2006
[DNS-SD] RFC 6763, DNS-Based Service Discovery, IETF, RFC, 2013
[DVB-TS.102.824] TS 102 824, Digital Video Broadcasting (DVB);Remote Management and Firmware Update System for DVB IP Services, ETSI, July 2008
[ETSIM2MFA] ETSI TS 102 690 v1.1.6, Machine-to-Machine Communications (M2M Functional Architecture), ETSI, June 2012
[G.9973] G.9973, Protocol for identifying home network topology, ITU-T, 2011
[HTML4.01] HTML 4.01 Specification, W3C
[ICSA-Baseline] ICSA Baseline Modular Firewall Certification Criteria, Baseline module - version 4.1, ICSA Labs, 2008
[ICSA-Firewall] ICSA Modular Firewall Certification Criteria, Required Services Security Policy - Small/Medium Business (SMB) Category module - version 4.0, ICSA Labs
[ICSA-Residential] ICSA Residential Modular Firewall Certification Criteria, Required Services Security Policy - Residential Category module - version 4.1, ICSA Labs, 2008
[IPDR-FTP] IPDR File Transfer Protocol, IPDR/File Transfer Protocol, TM Forum
[IPDR-SP] IPDR Streaming Protocol, IPDR Streaming Protocol (IPDR/SP) Specification, TM Forum
[IPDR-XDR] IPDR XDR Encoding Format, IPDR/XDR Encoding Format, TM Forum
[IPDR-XML] IPDR XML File Encoding Format, IPDR/XML File Encoding Format, TM Forum
[ISO-13818-6:1998] ISO/IEC 13818-6:1998, Information Technology - Generic coding of moving pictures and associated audio information - Part 6: Extensions for DSM-CC, ISO, 1998
[ITU-H.235.1] ITU-T H.235.1, H.323 security framework: Baseline security profile, ITU-T
[ITU-X.733] ITU X.733, Information technology - Open Systems Interconnection - Systems Management: Alarm reporting function, International Telecommunication Union, February 1992
[JJ-300.00] JJ-300.00, Home-network Topology Identifying Protocol, TTC, 2011
[JJ-300.01] JJ-300.01, The List of Device Categories, TTC, 2011
[OUI] Organizationally Unique Identifiers (OUIs)
[RED] References on RED (Random Early Detection) Queue Management
[RFC1035] RFC 1035, Domain Names - Implementation and Specification, IETF, RFC, 1987
[RFC1323] RFC 1323, TCP Extensions for High Performance, IETF, RFC, May 1992
[RFC2131] RFC 2131, Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol, IETF, RFC
[RFC2132] RFC 2132, DHCP Options and BOOTP Vendor Extensions, IETF, RFC
[RFC2198] RFC 2198, RTP Payload for Redundant Audio Data, IETF, RFC
[RFC2225] RFC 2225, Classical IP and ARP over ATM, IETF, RFC
[RFC2474] RFC 2474, Definition of the Differentiated Services Field (DS Field) in the IPv4 and IPv6 Headers, IETF, RFC
[RFC2516] RFC 2516, A Method for Transmitting PPP Over Ethernet (PPPoE), IETF, RFC
[RFC2581] RFC 2581, TCP Congestion Control, IETF, RFC, April 1999
[RFC2582] RFC 2582, The NewReno Modification to TCP's Fast Recovery Algorithm, IETF, RFC, April 1999
[RFC2616] RFC 2616, Hypertext Transfer Protocol -- HTTP/1.1, IETF, RFC, 1999
[RFC2634] RFC 2634, Enhanced Security Services for S/MIME, IETF, RFC
[RFC2662] RFC 2662, Definitions of Managed Objects for the ADSL Lines, IETF, RFC
[RFC2684] RFC 2684, Multiprotocol Encapsulation over ATM Adaptation Layer 5, IETF, RFC

[RFC3004] RFC 3004, The User Class Option for DHCP, IETF, RFC

[RFC3261] RFC 3261, SIP: Session Initiation Protocol, IETF, RFC, June 2002
